Confirmed independent of the headerless-reshaper feature (fails flag-off AND with the rc-op guard disabled). FIX DIRECTION: (a) `vec_elem_agg_drop_for_type_expr` Option arm — when the payload is shared (`shared_heap_type_for_type_expr(payload).is_some()`), return a tag-guarded Option-of-shared drop fn (if Some, rc-dec the boxed pointer); (b) ensure the primary `let v = Vec.new()` cleanup for a direct `Vec[shared]` routes through the element-aware rc-dec drop. Verify NO double-free (elements are Vec-owned; a subsequent read-walk over them is a borrow). Add a memory_sanitizer test for both shapes. FIXED (6eb7df42): the Option arm of vec_elem_agg_drop_for_type_expr now routes a shared payload to emit_option_drop_fn (tag-guarded — Vec elements use the TAGGED Option overlay {tag,payload}, not the niche pointer; a first niche attempt crashed at 0x1 = the tag). Full memory_sanitizer suite under Linux LSan now 606 passed / 0 failed. RESIDUAL, distinct symptom, filed as B-2026-07-11-36: a DIRECT Vec[shared struct] (no Option) still leaks the WHOLE Vec (buffer + element), unchanged by this fix.

tracker: add a codegen+interp for-loop reject (or adaptor support) + a `for x in stdin.lines().map(..)` regression
title: Adaptor chaining over `stdin.lines()` (`for x in stdin.lines().map(|r| r)` / `.filter(p)`) TYPECHECKS but silently iterates ZERO times under both `karac run` and `karac build` — a silent divergence introduced alongside the `stdin.lines()` slice (commit ae9e4f8). ROOT: `stdin.lines()` returns the new `StdinLines` iterator type, which the typechecker's iterator-adaptor machinery accepts as an iterable (it has an `Item` mapping), so `.map()`/`.filter()` typecheck and produce an adaptor iterator over `StdinLines`. But the interpreter's for-loop drain only recognizes a bare `Value::StdinLines` iterable (eval_expr.rs), and codegen's `compile_for` only intercepts a bare `stdin.lines()` receiver (`compile_for_stdin_lines`); an adaptor-wrapped chain falls through to the map/filter fusion, which peels to a base it cannot lower for a `StdinLines` source → the silent `_ =>` no-op (the same class as B-2026-07-11-18 for-over-chain). Directly iterating `for line in stdin.lines()` works correctly in both backends (verified over empty/no-trailing-newline/blank-line inputs); ONLY the adaptor-chained form is affected. IMPACT: low — the common form (direct iteration + match/filter inside the body) is correct; adaptor chaining on stdin is niche. FIX DIRECTION: either (a) support `StdinLines` as a fusion base source in both backends (the pull-loop equivalent of the Vec-storage fusion), or (b) reject an adaptor chain whose peeled base is `stdin.lines()` LOUDLY at typecheck/codegen/interp with a 'chain filters/maps inside the for-body instead' message — fail-closed, no silent wrong answer. Filed per the never-route-around-it rule; the direct-iteration surface (the shipped deliverable) is unaffected.
resolution: Fixed fail-closed at typecheck: `StdinLines` (`stdin.lines()`) and `LinesIter` (`BufReader.lines()`) are opaque line-iterator markers with NO surface methods (iteration is `for line in <iter>` only), so an explicit guard in `infer_method_call` (src/typechecker/expr_method_call.rs, right after the Iterator/Peekable/Range adaptor dispatch) rejects ANY `.method()` on either type with a helpful `NoMethodFound` diagnostic pointing at the working shape (iterate directly, filter/map inside the loop body; items are `Result[String, IoError]`). This also closes the PRE-EXISTING `LinesIter` twin: `br.lines().map(..)` used to typecheck then silently iterate zero times (the same silent-no-op class), and now errors loudly too. Direct for-loop iteration does not go through method dispatch, so `for line in stdin.lines()` / `br.lines()` is unaffected. Regression: tests/typechecker.rs::test_stdin_lines_adaptor_rejected + test_bufreader_lines_adaptor_rejected (both assert the loud rejection); the existing bare-iteration tests pin the control path.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: self-review while shipping stdin.lines() (phase-8 line 773)

fix: a663328 (read leg) + d3a72bb (return-field-element leg) + 783cf63 (PUSH leg) + 757f16b (RETURN-OWNED-`T`-PARAM leg: mono tail-return deep-copy of an owned-vecstr param, mirroring the non-generic path + element-aware mono-mangle disambiguation for builtin-collection whole-type-params). ALL FOUR LEGS FIXED.
tracker: FIXED legs: tests/codegen.rs::e2e_generic_container_field_index_read_resolves_element (read, a663328); tests/codegen.rs::e2e_return_field_index_element_clones + tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_return_field_index_element_no_double_free (return-field-element, d3a72bb); tests/codegen.rs::e2e_generic_container_method_push_noncopy_element + tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_generic_container_method_push_no_leak (PUSH, 783cf63). RETURN-OWNED-`T`-PARAM leg (3b, 757f16b): tests/codegen.rs::e2e_return_owned_generic_param_no_double_free + tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_return_owned_generic_param_no_double_free; also pins the element-aware mono-mangle disambiguation (test_slice_8w/8x driver-mono symbols updated). ALL LEGS FIXED. Discriminators: examples/heap.kara (i64, works); the non-generic B-2026-07-11-32 probes; B-2026-07-11-31 (generic method dispatch).
title: A GENERIC container over a NON-COPY element (`Heap[String]`, `H[T]{xs:Vec[T]}`) was broken across several DIRECT-field-access legs. ALL FIXED: (1) field-index READ `h.xs[i]`/`self.xs[i]` mis-resolved the element to the i64 default (a663328); (3a) RETURNING a field-rooted index element (`fn get(ref self)->T{self.xs[i]}`) aliased the container's element (d3a72bb); (2) generic method PUSH `self.xs.push(x)` MOVED the caller's buffer (garbage) then, once deep-copied, LEAKED the element buffers (783cf63: owned-`T` deep-copy classification + PER-MONOMORPH struct-drop `__karac_drop_struct_S$String`); (3b) returning an owned `T` PARAM (`fn echo[T](x:T)->T{x}`) double-freed (757f16b: mono tail-return deep-copy). Fixing (3b) also uncovered + closed a latent mono-MANGLE COLLISION: String / Vec[i64] / Vec[String] whole-type-params all lower to `{ptr,i64,i64}` and shared one `$struct` mono body. Interpreter (oracle) correct throughout. Sibling of B-2026-07-11-31.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: Surfaced extending the B-2026-07-11-32 non-Copy-swap fix to a generic `Heap[String]` dogfood: a generic struct's FIELD-rooted index read of a non-Copy element reads garbage. — For `struct H[T] { xs: Vec[T] }` instantiated `H[String]`, a direct field-rooted index read `self.xs[i]` (or `h.xs[i]` in a caller) inside a monomorphized method reads the element with the WRONG width/type: the generic field `Vec[T]` element is resolved to the i64 unknown-name DEFAULT rather than the concrete `String`, so codegen loads 8 bytes and treats them as a scalar instead of loading the 24-byte {ptr,len,cap} String header. REPRO (interp `hello hello`; native prints a garbage integer then corrupt bytes): `struct H[T] { xs: Vec[T] }  impl[T] H[T] { fn new() -> H[T] { H { xs: Vec.new() } }  fn push(mut ref self, x: T) { self.xs.push(x); }  fn showfirst(ref self) { println(self.xs[0]); } }  fn main() { let mut h: H[String] = H.new(); h.push(f"hello"); h.push(f"world"); println(h.xs[0]); h.showfirst(); }`. WORKS for `H[i64]` (i64 IS the default — examples/heap.kara ships an i64 heap). This is the DIRECT-field-read sibling of B-2026-07-11-31 (which resolved the generic-method dispatch surface, add/get/pop, but not the raw `self.field[i]` read type). It BLOCKS a generic `Heap[String]` end to end (the B-2026-07-11-32 non-Copy swap fix is otherwise complete and clean for non-generic containers). LIKELY FIX AREA: the field-rooted index read (compile_index's FieldAccess arm / lower_field_access_ptr element-type resolution) must resolve the struct field's `Vec[T]` element through the active monomorph type_subst_names (as vec_index_elem_type_expr's new FieldAccess arm already does for the clone path), not fall back to the i64 default. Filed on discovery per the never-route-around-it rule; the B-2026-07-11-32 clone helper guards on the read value's LLVM type so this mis-read cannot escalate to a misaligned-copy abort.
resolution: RETURN-OWNED-`T`-PARAM leg FIXED (757f16b). ROOT: `compile_mono_function`'s tail return (src/codegen/mono.rs) did only the Identifier-tail MOVE suppression (`suppress_cleanup_for_-tail_return` zeros the local param cap) but SKIPPED the owned-vecstr retaining-consume DEEP-COPY that the non-generic `compile_function` applies at its tail (functions.rs, via `maybe_defensive_copy_param_arg` on the final expr). Owned String/Vec by-value params are caller-retains (they land in `owned_vecstr_params`, never a callee-side `track_vec_var`), so the callee must COPY whatever owned param it returns — else it hands back the caller's moved-in buffer, which the caller frees a SECOND time (`fn echo[T](x:T)->T{x}` over a fresh String arg: main freed it once as `a` and once as the f-string temp → `free(): double free`). IR-CONFIRMED: the non-generic `ff` emits a `dcopy` (malloc+memcpy) block on return; the mono `echo$struct` returned `x1` directly. FIX: apply `maybe_defensive_copy_param_arg` to the mono tail-return value BEFORE `suppress_cleanup_for_tail_return` (the copy reads the param cap the suppression zeros). i64 (POD) is unchanged (the copy self-gates on `owned_vecstr_params`). ALSO FIXED (a latent, pre-existing bug the copy EXPOSED): a mono-MANGLE COLLISION. `String`, `Vec[i64]`, and `Vec[String]` as a WHOLE generic type param (`echo[T](x: T)`) all lower to the opaque `{ptr,i64,i64}` LLVM shape, and `mangle_mono_name` (src/codegen/mono.rs) disambiguates the `struct` token by concrete NAME only for USER struct/enum — builtins keep the opaque `$struct`, so all three collapsed onto ONE `echo$struct` body. Benign while the body only MOVED the value (element-agnostic); a MISCOMPILE once the tail deep-copies with the FIRST instantiation's element stride (`echo[String]` then `echo[Vec[i64]]`: the Vec copy ran String's i8 stride → 3-byte under-copy → UB — JIT read garbage, native happened to read 10, oracle correct). FIX: element-aware disambiguation — typechecker records `type_to_mono_mangle_token` per generic-call param (`Vec_i64` / `Vec_String` / `String`) in the new `call_type_subs_mangle` (sibling of the head-only `call_type_subs`), threaded through lowering → codegen; `compile_generic_call`'s `append_collection_type_param_mangle` appends `$<param>_ct_<token>` for a param whose HEAD is a `{ptr,i64,i64}` builtin collection (String/Vec/VecDeque, exact head match, not user-typed), so each instantiation is a distinct symbol (`echo$struct$T_ct_String` / `$T_ct_Vec_i64` / `$T_ct_Vec_String`) with its own correctly-strided body. Map/Set (single-`ptr` handle) and scalars mangle distinctly already and are untouched; the coroutine state-machine driver monos (`kara.state.driver$struct` per test_slice_8w/8x) pick up the same suffix (updated in-place). VERIFIED: interp(oracle)==JIT==native across return of String / Vec[i64] / Vec[String] / i64 / owned-var / chained `echo(echo(..))`; full codegen (2198) + memory_sanitizer (624, LSan) + non-codegen (7789) suites green; fmt+clippy clean. Tests: tests/codegen.rs::e2e_return_owned_generic_param_no_double_free, tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_return_owned_generic_param_no_double_free. --- PRIOR LEGS --- PUSH LEG FIXED (783cf63). KEY REFRAME: `karac run` JITs by default under `--features llvm` (`KARAC_RUN_JIT`), so the 'interpreter garbage' seen while probing was JIT (codegen) garbage — the tree-walk interpreter oracle (`KARAC_RUN_JIT=0`) was correct all along, matching the ledger's codegen-only framing. TWO coupled codegen defects, both closed: (A) CORRECTNESS — src/codegen/mono.rs param prologue registered `x: T` off the UNRESOLVED bare `T` (`register_var_from_type_expr` saw `Path("T")`), so a `x:T` (T=String) param never entered `vec_elem_types`/`owned_vecstr_params` and the retaining `self.xs.push(x)` MOVED the caller's buffer instead of deep-copying it — every method-pushed element read back garbage from the caller (`Box[String].add(f"apple")`). FIX: resolve `registration_te` through the active `subst_monomorph_type_params` before registration (a no-op outside a monomorph). (B) LEAK — once (A) deep-copies, the element buffers leaked because `emit_struct_drop_synthesis` (src/codegen/synth_drop.rs) synthesized ONE drop fn per struct NAME and resolved the `Vec[T]` field element from bare `T`, never draining the concrete `Vec[String]` (9x under `asan_generic_assoc_fn_vec_field_no_leak`). FIX: per-MONOMORPH struct-drop synthesis — `emit_struct_drop_synthesis_mono(struct_name, subst)` mangles a distinct symbol per instantiation (`__karac_drop_struct_Box$String` vs `$i64`) and applies the `param->concrete` subst when resolving each `Vec[T]` field element; `track_struct_var_inst` threads the binding's recorded instantiation (`enum_inst_var_types[var]`) from the let-site. Same LLVM struct type across instantiations (the Vec header is `{ptr,len,cap}` regardless of element), so only the drop-fn BODY differs — and the distinct symbols are REQUIRED for safety: a name-shared drop running the String drain over an `i64` Vec would `free` each i64 as a bogus `{ptr,len,cap}`. The caller-side fresh-owned-temp cleanup the earlier diagnosis expected (leg (b)) proved UNNEEDED — the f-string acc's own `track_vec_var` cleanup already frees the original temp (as the IR trace concluded); no caller-side change was required. VERIFIED: interp(oracle)==JIT==native across push+read, a Box[String]/Box[i64] coexistence program, and full-drain loops; full codegen suite (2192) + memory_sanitizer suite (623, LSan) green; fmt+clippy(--all-targets --features llvm) clean. Tests: tests/codegen.rs::e2e_generic_container_method_push_noncopy_element, tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_generic_container_method_push_no_leak. examples/heap.kara (i64) unchanged; the push leg now supports String-element generic containers. --- PRIOR LEGS --- TWO legs FIXED. READ leg (a663328): resolve the field's `Vec[T]` element to the container's concrete instantiation before synth registration, via `resolve_generic_field_te` (enum_inst_type_of_expr primary, type_subst_names fallback), applied at `lower_field_access_ptr` and `vec_index_elem_type_expr`'s FieldAccess arm — a literal-built generic container now reads non-Copy elements with the right stride. RETURN-FIELD-ELEMENT leg (d3a72bb): `compile_tail_final_expr` (src/codegen/stmts.rs) now deep-clones a FieldAccess-object element index at the fn tail (`fn get(ref self)->T{self.xs[i]}` / `fn getf(h: ref H,i)->T{h.xs[i]}`) — a `ref self`/`ref Struct` can't move the element out, so the returned owned `T` must be a clone; the bare-`v[i]` return already yields an independent value, so only the field-rooted shape needed it. REMAINING OPEN LEGS with the full in-session diagnosis: (2) GENERIC PUSH — root at src/codegen/mono.rs ~1502-1506: the mono param prologue registers a param's String/Vec side-tables from the UNRESOLVED bare `T` (`register_var_from_type_expr` sees `Path("T")`; `is_string_type_expr`/`extract_vec_elem_type` match the literal outer name only), so a `x:T` (T=String) param never enters `vec_elem_types`/`owned_vecstr_params`; the retaining `Vec.push(x)` then MOVES the caller's buffer instead of deep-copying it (AK: `let s=f"hi"; hs.add(s)` double-frees). Resolving `registration_te` via `subst_monomorph_type_params` there DOES make a full `Heap[String]` (push+sift+pop) work end to end (verified: interp==native `apple banana cherry date pear`), BUT it regresses `asan_generic_assoc_fn_vec_field_no_leak`: once the param deep-copies, a FRESH f-string temp arg (`s.push(f"row-{r}")`) leaks its original buffer (9x10-byte accs in main) — the deep-copy is essential (dropping it reverts strheap to garbage), so the fix ALSO needs the caller-side fresh-owned-temp cleanup for a generic (inline-mono) call to match the non-generic path (the non-generic sibling of that exact test is ASAN-clean; it frees via the f-string acc's own track_vec_var cleanup). IR-TRACED to the true root: it is NOT an acc-suppression — with the deep-copy fix, `main` DOES free each f-string acc (a per-push `cleanup.free` gated on `cap>0`), and `Vec.push` deep-copies the arg into `items`; the LEAK is the DEEP-COPIED element buffers, because the GENERIC STRUCT DROP does not free them. `emit_struct_drop_synthesis` (src/codegen/synth_drop.rs) caches ONE drop fn per struct NAME (`struct_drop_fns[struct_name]` -> `__karac_drop_struct_S`) and resolves its `Vec[T]` field element from the DECLARED `struct_field_type_exprs` (bare `T`), so `vec_elem_agg_drop_for_type_expr(T)` yields no per-element drain — the `Vec[String]` field's String buffers are never freed. The drop fn is shared across `S[String]`/`S[i64]` (same name, same `{ptr,len,cap}` Vec LLVM type), so it CANNOT free instantiation-specific heap elements without being MONOMORPHIZED per instantiation (a mangled `__karac_drop_struct_S$String` keyed on struct name + type args, with the StructDrop registration sites threading the concrete mono type). Before the deep-copy fix this was masked: push MOVED the arg, so `main`'s acc `cleanup.free` freed the sole buffer and the un-freeing drop was a no-op net-zero; the deep-copy makes `items` own an independent copy that the name-shared drop then orphans. So the push leg = mono.rs ~1502 deep-copy classification (easy) + PER-MONOMORPH struct-drop synthesis (architectural — the hard core). (3b) RETURN-OWNED-`T`-PARAM — `fn echo[T](x:T)->T{x}` double-frees a returned owned heap PARAM (String/Vec) while the non-generic `fn f(x:String)->String{x}` is clean; the mono tail-return suppression (`suppress_cleanup_for_tail_return`, mono.rs ~1609) does not neutralise the returned param for a generic mono (likely the same bare-`T`-not-in-`vec_elem_types` root as leg 2, since `suppress_source_vec_cleanup_for_arg` no-ops for a name not in `vec_elem_types`). FIX SHAPE for the open core (one dedicated slice): (a) resolve the mono param registration at mono.rs ~1502 (deep-copy classification — also fixes 3b's suppression) + (b) free a fresh owned-heap temp arg to a generic call caller-side. BLOCKS a `push`-built generic `Heap[String]`; examples/heap.kara stays i64-element until it lands.

title: Vec[shared struct] (direct, non-Option) leaks the whole Vec — buffer + element not freed
detail: SOURCE NOTE: repro during B-2026-07-11-33 fix — A `let mut v: Vec[<shared struct>] = Vec.new(); v.push(node);` leaks the Vec DATA BUFFER (karac_realloc_or_panic, ~48 B) and the element node (indirect) at scope exit — macOS `leaks` shows 2 leaks. Distinct from B-2026-07-11-33 (which was Vec[Option[shared]] element drop): the let-site DOES dispatch element-aware drop (agg_drop=true via emit_vec_elem_rc_dec_fn, the direct-shared path at src/codegen/runtime.rs vec_elem_agg_drop_for_type_expr line ~1746), yet the whole Vec (buffer included) leaks — so the FreeVecBuffer drain isn't reclaiming this Vec (buffer free not firing), a different root cause than the Option-payload dispatch gap. Vec[Option[shared]] and the reshaper family are unaffected (green under LSan). Repro: c5 in the B-33 investigation. Not yet root-caused past this; less common than the Option-wrapped shape.
resolution: NOT A REAL LEAK — closed as a macOS `leaks`-tool artifact. Under the authoritative Linux LeakSanitizer the single-element Vec[shared] and Vec[Option[shared]] shapes are CLEAN (new test memory_sanitizer::asan_vec_of_shared_push_drop_singleton passes; full suite 612+ green). macOS `leaks` over-reported the `karac_realloc_or_panic` Vec buffer as leaked because the tool doesn't track that custom-allocator wrapper's frees; LSan intercepts at malloc and sees it freed. The push-count 'signal' (1 leaks / 2 clean on macOS) was `leaks` under-reporting the 2-element case, not a real difference. Lesson: use the docker Linux-LSan harness as the leak gate for Vec/shared, not macOS `leaks`.

fix: The method-call by-value argument path (`compile_method_call`, src/codegen/method_call.rs) now calls `suppress_inline_option_result_binding_move(&a.value)` for an owned by-value arg — the same caller-slot-nulling the free-fn path (`compile_call`, call_dispatch.rs:1535) already applied but the method path omitted. A moved inline-heap `Option[String]` / `Result` / boxed-enum binding now zeroes its caller slot, so the caller's scope-exit `FreeInlineOptionPayload` no longer re-frees the payload the callee's match arm already dropped. Gated OUT of a return-passthrough via `find_function_ast(qualified)` + `fn_returns_param(pidx)` (the method's `self` is param 0, so source arg `i` maps to declared param `i+1`); by-ref args never reach the by-value tail (every `is_ref` arm `continue`s earlier), so no borrow gate is needed, and the helper self-guards on the inline/boxed payload sets so shared `Option[shared T]` and untracked args are untouched. Regression: tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_option_heap_moved_from_recursive_shared_enum_into_mut_ref_self_method_no_double_free (LeakSanitizer OFF via the new `assert_no_double_free_leaks_allowed` helper — this shape ALSO carries the separate, pre-existing leak B-2026-07-11-39, so a full-leak gate would mask the double-free).
tracker: worked around in selfhost by inlining the Option match (never passing Option[String] by value to the helper); repro below
title: Passing an `Option[String]` moved out of a RECURSIVE shared-enum node BY VALUE to a `mut ref self` method double-frees the payload under codegen (JIT+AOT `free(): double free detected in tcache 2`); interpreter correct. Passing the same Option by REF, or inlining the `match` at the call site, is clean.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: Surfaced porting loop-label resolution into the self-hosted resolver (Phase 12 resolver hardening): a `continue <label>` handler that passed the AST node's `Option[String]` label BY VALUE to a `mut ref self` helper double-freed the label payload at runtime. — A `mut ref self` method parameter of type `Option[String]`, where the argument was just moved out of a variant payload of a shared enum that has a RECURSIVE variant, is dropped TWICE: once inside the callee (after the `Some(l)` arm consumes/borrows `l`) and again when the caller's source node is dropped — the by-value arg transfer of the Option payload doesn't null the caller's slot, so both the callee's frame drop and the shared node's drop free the same String. AOT + JIT corrupt the heap (glibc `free(): double free detected in tcache 2`); `karac run --interp` is CORRECT, so it is a run/build divergence with NO diagnostic. DISCRIMINATORS: (1) passing the Option BY REF (`label: ref Option[String]`, caller passes `n.label`) is CLEAN. (2) INLINING the `match label { Some(l) => ... }` at the call site (no helper) is CLEAN. (3) a SINGLE-variant shared enum (no recursive sibling) does NOT reproduce — the second, recursive variant (`Wrap(WrapNode { inner: Expr })`) changes the enum's drop glue and is required. (4) a free FUNCTION (not a `mut ref self` method) taking the Option by value does NOT reproduce; the `mut ref self` receiver is required. REPRO (build/JIT abort; interp prints `hits: 1`): `shared enum Expr { Cont(ContNode), Wrap(WrapNode) }  struct ContNode { label: Option[String] }  struct WrapNode { inner: Expr }  struct R { labels: Vec[String], hits: i64 }  impl R { fn in_scope(ref self, name: ref String) -> bool { let mut i = 0; loop { if i >= self.labels.len() { return false; } if self.labels[i] == name { return true; } i = i + 1; } }  fn label_ref(mut ref self, label: Option[String]) { match label { Some(l) => { if not self.in_scope(l) { self.hits = self.hits + 1; } } None => {} } }  fn walk(mut ref self, e: Expr) { match e { Cont(n) => { let ContNode { label } = n; self.label_ref(label); } Wrap(n) => { let WrapNode { inner } = n; self.walk(inner); } } } }  fn main() { let mut r = R { labels: Vec.new(), hits: 0 }; let e = Expr.Cont(ContNode { label: Some("nope".to_string()) }); r.walk(e); println(r.hits.to_string()); }`. LIKELY FIX AREA: the by-value argument-transfer path for an `Option[<heap>]` payload (the `emit_option_value_clone_fn` / caller-retains-copy boundary, cf. B-2026-07-10-4 residual + B-2026-07-11-21) must null / move-out the caller's Option slot when the arg is a moved (not copied) owned value, so the shared node's later drop does not re-free the payload. Related family: B-2026-07-10-4 (token-view Option[String] entry-copy double-free), B-2026-07-11-21 (reused Option[shared] across consuming calls).
resolution: Fixed 2026-07-11 — mirrored `compile_call`'s by-value Option/Result/boxed move-suppression into `compile_method_call`, with a method-aware return-passthrough guard. Confirmed: the ledger repro built AOT now prints the correct result on every run (was `free(): double free detected`); interpreter unchanged. Note: the residual steady-state leak in this shape is the separate B-2026-07-11-39, not this defect.

tracker: codegen for a Result[Vec[String], IoError]-returning builtin; e2e read_lines under karac build
title: `fs.read_lines(path) -> Result[Vec[String], IoError]` is INTERPRETER-ONLY at v1 — no codegen. Under `karac build` (and default `karac run`, which JITs) it is rejected LOUDLY: the lowercase `fs.read_lines()` form hits the `compile_ambient_ffi` catch-all ('ambient resource method not yet lowered (interpreter-only)') and the capitalized `FileSystem.read_lines()` form hits an explicit guard in `src/codegen/assoc_call.rs` (added with the feature) pointing at `karac run` / `fs.read_to_string + String.split`. Runs correctly via `karac run --interp` / `KARAC_RUN_JIT=0` (and the tree-walk interpreter in tests) with exact Rust `str::lines()` semantics (CRLF-stripped, no trailing empty). Consistent with the existing `BufReader.lines()` precedent (also interpreter-only, no codegen) but strictly better — loud gate + a `--interp` hint rather than a silent under-build no-op. FIX DIRECTION: lower the builtin — a runtime extern `karac_runtime_fs_read_lines(path, out: *mut KaracVec) -> i32` building a `Vec[String]` the same way `karac_runtime_env_args_into` does, then codegen a `Result[Vec[String], IoError]` around it (Ok wraps the loaded `{ptr,len,cap}` Vec aggregate; Err from the error_kind — mirror `lower_kara_io_result`'s error path + the `try_with_capacity` Result[Vec[T],_] Ok construction), plus the interpreter parity that already exists. Then remove both loud gates and add an e2e build==run test. Not urgent (interpreter path is correct + the gate is loud, not silent).
resolution: Fixed 2026-07-11 — lowered the builtin end to end. RUNTIME: a new extern `karac_runtime_fs_read_lines(out_io: *mut KaracIoResult, out_vec: *mut KaracVec, path_ptr, path_len)` (runtime/src/file.rs) does `std::fs::read_to_string` then `content.lines().collect()`, building a KaracVec of `RuntimeKaracString` (each own alloc, cap==len; empty file → the canonical `{null,0,0}` so cleanup is a no-op) and writing `ok(0)`; on error it writes an empty vec + `err(&e)`. Two out-params (not a by-value 32-byte KaracIoResult return) keep the ABI trivial, same rationale as the File open-family. CODEGEN: `compile_fs_read_lines`/`_val` (src/codegen/file.rs) alloca a KaracIoResult slot + a `vec_struct_type()` slot, call the extern, branch on `error_kind == 0`: Ok loads the filled KaracVec and wraps it via `build_nonshared_enum_value("Result","Ok",[vec])` (a Vec is 3 words, packs into Result's 4 payload words); Err rebuilds the IoError exactly like `lower_kara_io_result`'s Err arm (Err tag 0, IoError variant tag = error_kind−1, Other(String) msg ptr/len/len). Extern declared in Codegen::new (src/codegen.rs). DISPATCH: capitalized `FileSystem.read_lines` in assoc_call.rs and lowercase ambient `fs.read_lines` in method_call.rs `compile_ambient_ffi` both route to it — the two loud gates removed. The `Vec[String]` element-drop is unchanged (the standard `vec_elem_types` String-elem free), verified leak-free. Regression: tests/codegen.rs::{test_e2e_fs_read_lines_splits_file_into_vec, test_e2e_lowercase_fs_read_lines_question_unwrap, test_e2e_fs_read_lines_missing_file_returns_err, test_ir_read_lines_dispatches_to_runtime_ffi} + tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_fs_read_lines_vec_string_elements_freed (30× read loop, LSan-clean). Build==run parity + valgrind 0 leaks confirmed by hand.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: phase-8 line 773 — shipping fs.read_lines() interpreter-first

fix: Root cause: the recursive `shared enum` rc-drop destructor (`emit_shared_enum_rc_drop_fn`, synth_drop.rs) decides which variants to drop via `field_is_walkable`, whose struct-payload arm gates on `struct_owns_shared_field || type_expr_has_drop_heap`. `type_expr_has_drop_heap` has a DELIBERATE `Option | Result => false` blind spot (its copy-side callers depend on it), so a payload struct whose only heap is an `Option[String]` / `Option[Vec[T]]` field read as heapless -> the whole variant was judged non-walkable, got no drop block, and its boxed payload + Some payload leaked. The recursive variant (`Wrap(WrapNode{inner:Expr})`) is required only because it makes the enum need the generated destructor at all (a single-variant enum returns None and the payload is reclaimed via the struct's own value-drop, which DOES handle Option[String] via `FieldDrop::OptionInline`). Fix (3 coordinated sites in synth_drop.rs, mirroring B-2026-07-11-33): (1) `field_is_walkable` struct arm and (2) `emit_shared_enum_field_drop` struct arm both OR in the Option-aware companion predicate `te_owns_option_heap_payload` so the variant is walked; (3) `emit_nested_struct_shared_rc_decs_ex` (the payload-struct field walker) gains an `Option[<inline-heap>]` arm (sibling of its existing `Option[shared]` and plain-`String` arms) that frees the Some payload via `emit_option_drop_fn(payload)`, gated on `owns_buffer_free` (RC-box path only; the value-drop path's `__karac_drop_struct_<S>` already frees it). Covers Option[String] and Option[Vec[T]]. The sibling `shared struct` + Option[String] shape was probed and is already leak-clean (no change needed there). Regression: tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_recursive_shared_enum_option_heap_payload_field_no_leak (full LSan). Also upgraded the B-2026-07-11-37 regression from its LeakSanitizer-off gate back to the full-leak `assert_clean_asan_run` (that shape shared this leak; now fully clean).
tracker: minimal repro below reproduces with a bare create+drop; orthogonal to B-2026-07-11-37 — present with no match, no move, no method call. The B-2026-07-11-37 regression test runs with LeakSanitizer OFF to avoid masking that double-free with this leak.
title: Dropping a recursive `shared enum` whose variant payload struct holds an `Option[String]` (or `Option[<inline-heap>]`) field LEAKS the `Some` payload under codegen: the shared enum's rc-drop glue never frees the boxed/inline heap payload of a struct-field Option. Reproduces with a bare `let e = Expr.Cont(ContNode { label: Some(s) })` create-and-drop — NO match, NO move, NO method call — so it is independent of the by-value-arg double-free B-2026-07-11-37. Native run is clean (leaks do not crash); the Linux CI memory-sanitizer job (LSan) is the gate.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: Surfaced while fixing B-2026-07-11-37 (the by-value-arg double-free in the same shape): with that double-free fixed, ASAN/LSan still reports a steady-state leak, and it reproduces with NO method call at all. — MINIMAL REPRO (LSan reports ~2 leaked allocations per loop iteration — the Some payload's `String` buffer and its containing heap allocation): `shared enum Expr { Cont(ContNode), Wrap(WrapNode) }  struct ContNode { label: Option[String] }  struct WrapNode { inner: Expr }  fn main() { let mut round = 0; while round < 50 { let e = Expr.Cont(ContNode { label: Some("payload-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx".to_string()) }); println(round.to_string()); round = round + 1; } }`. DISCRIMINATORS: (1) the RECURSIVE second variant (`Wrap(WrapNode { inner: Expr })`) is what routes `Expr` through the boxed/rc drop-glue path where the leak lives; a single-variant shared enum uses different glue. (2) The leak is independent of how the payload is later consumed: by-ref, by-value, method, and free-function variants of the B-2026-07-11-37 shape ALL leak the same ~2 allocs/iteration, and the bare create+drop above (no consumer at all) leaks identically. (3) `karac run --interp` is presumed correct (the interpreter frees the payload); build/AOT leaks — a run/build divergence with no diagnostic. LIKELY FIX AREA: the shared-enum destructor / rc-drop glue (`emit_enum_drop_fn` / the shared-enum drop path in src/codegen) must recurse into a struct payload field of type `Option[<inline-heap>]` and tag-guarded-free its `Some` payload, the way `vec_elem_agg_drop_for_type_expr` was taught to for `Vec[Option[shared]]` (B-2026-07-11-33). Related family: B-2026-07-11-33 (Vec[Option[shared]] element drop leak, fixed), B-2026-07-11-37 (the by-value-arg double-free sibling in this exact shape, fixed). Impact: this is the canonical AST-node shape (an enum node whose payload carries an optional owned string), heavily used by the self-hosted compiler port, so the leak is broad.
resolution: Fixed 2026-07-11 — taught the recursive shared-enum drop glue the Option[<inline-heap>] payload-field case (walkability gates + a walker arm), mirroring the B-2026-07-11-33 vec-element Option fix. Confirmed leak-free under Linux LSan; the whole shared_enum ASAN family (23 tests) stays green.

tracker: FIXED (844e3b9): tests/codegen.rs::e2e_struct_field_by_ref_to_free_fn (interp==JIT==native for ref Vec[i64]/Vec[String] field reads + mut ref field mutation) + tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_struct_field_by_ref_to_free_fn_no_double_free (ASAN+LSan clean over a loop). Regression guard: borrowed_option_struct_payload_field_read_is_correct (the Option[shared] field arg the gate must NOT intercept). selfhost `struct_id_of` self-method workaround no longer required.
title: Passing a struct FIELD (`self.names`) BY REF to a FREE function double-frees the field's Vec under codegen (AOT `free(): double free detected in tcache 2`); interpreter correct. A LOCAL Vec passed by ref to the same free fn is CLEAN, and a `ref self` METHOD scanning `self.field` directly is CLEAN — only the self-field-to-free-fn-by-ref path corrupts.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: Surfaced building the self-hosted typechecker's struct-def table (Phase 12 Slice 6): a `ref self` method passing `self.<Vec field>` by ref to a free function double-freed the field's Vec. — Inside a `ref self` method, passing `self.<field>` (a `Vec[String]` field) by ref to a FREE function whose parameter is `ref Vec[String]` double-frees the field's backing Vec at scope exit under AOT codegen (glibc `free(): double free detected in tcache 2`). `karac run --interp` is CORRECT, so it is a run/build divergence with NO diagnostic. DISCRIMINATORS: (1) passing a plain LOCAL `Vec` (not a self-field) by ref to the SAME free function is CLEAN. (2) Reading `self.field` via a `ref self` METHOD that scans it directly (no free-fn hop) is CLEAN. Only the combination self-field + by-ref-arg + free (non-method) callee corrupts — the caller's `ref` to the field aliases the field, yet the field is still dropped by the receiver's normal field-drop AND (apparently) freed again along the borrowed-arg path. REPRO (build/JIT abort; interp prints `1`): `fn scan(names: ref Vec[String], name: ref String) -> i64 { let mut i = 0; loop { if i >= names.len() { return -1; } if names[i] == name { return i; } i = i + 1; } }  struct T { names: Vec[String] }  impl T { fn find(ref self, q: ref String) -> i64 { scan(self.names, q) } }  fn main() { let mut t = T { names: Vec.new() }; t.names.push("a".to_string()); t.names.push("b".to_string()); let q = "b".to_string(); println(t.find(q).to_string()); }`. WORKAROUND (used in `selfhost/src/typechecker.kara`): make the scan a method on `self` (`fn struct_id_of(ref self, name: ref String)` scanning `self.struct_names` directly), never passing the field to a free fn. LIKELY FIX AREA: the by-ref argument path for a field place-expression (`self.field`) must borrow (not move/copy-then-free) the field's aggregate; the receiver's field-drop is the sole owner. Related family: B-2026-07-11-37 (Option[String] by-value param), B-2026-07-11-21 (reused Option[shared]).
resolution: FIXED (844e3b9). ROOT: the `ref`/`mut ref` argument path in `compile_call` (src/codegen/call_dispatch.rs) had in-place-borrow fast-paths for a local `Identifier` (pass the var's slot pointer via `get_data_ptr`), a `vec[idx]` element, and a borrow-returning call — but NONE for a struct FIELD place (`self.names` / `obj.field`). So a field arg fell through to the RVALUE path, which shallow-copies the arg value into a `ref_rvalue_arg{i}` temp and queues a scope-exit cleanup via `queue_ref_rvalue_arg_cleanup`. For a `Vec[String]` field that cleanup drains each element's String buffer AND frees the `{ptr,len,cap}` data buffer — but the temp's header shares the SAME `data` pointer as the field (it is a shallow copy), and the receiver still owns the field (its own field-drop / the owner's scope-exit `StructDrop` frees it), so the temp's free double-frees the shared buffer (`scan(self.names, q)` → glibc `free(): double free detected in tcache 2`; JIT+native abort, interp correct — a silent run/build divergence). A LOCAL `Vec` took the Identifier fast-path (true borrow, no free) and was clean; only the field place fell through. FIX: intercept a FieldAccess place arg to a `ref`/`mut ref` param and borrow the field IN PLACE — `lower_field_access_ptr` GEPs the field off the (deref'd, for a `ref self`) receiver pointer, and that pointer is passed directly (no temp, no cleanup), exactly like the Identifier fast-path passes a local's slot. Normalizes a `SelfValue` object to `Identifier("self")` first (the shape `lower_field_access_ptr` expects). GATED to the confirmed double-free class (field LLVM type == the `{ptr,len,cap}` vec-struct, i.e. Vec/String): an `Option[shared T]` field arg keeps the existing path because it needs the `share_option_shared_field_ref_for_arg` RC-inc (a first, too-broad cut bypassed it and broke a recursive `any_negative(n.next)` borrow — caught by `borrowed_option_struct_payload_field_read_is_correct`); niche/enum fields aren't plain owned buffers and scalar fields were never double-freed, so both fall through unchanged. A chained (`a.b.c`) or otherwise unrecognized field shape returns None/Err from `lower_field_access_ptr` and also falls through, so behavior there is unchanged. VERIFIED: interp(oracle)==JIT==native for the repro AND a broad probe (a `ref Vec[i64]` field read, a `ref Vec[String]` field scan, and a `mut ref Vec[i64]` field whose in-place mutation correctly propagates back through the borrow), plus the local-Vec control. Full codegen (2202) + memory_sanitizer (625, LSan) + non-codegen suites green; fmt+clippy clean. Tests: tests/codegen.rs::e2e_struct_field_by_ref_to_free_fn, tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_struct_field_by_ref_to_free_fn_no_double_free. selfhost's `struct_id_of` workaround (scanning `self.field` as a method to avoid the free-fn hop) is no longer necessary, though left in place. NOTE: a Map/Set or owned-struct field passed by ref may be a latent sibling of this class (not exercised here); the vec-struct gate leaves them on the existing path.

tracker: heap-T OnceLock/OnceCell set/get codegen: move-suppression + rejected-value drop + Option/Result payload boxing
title: `OnceLock[T]`/`OnceCell[T]` `set`/`get` codegen supports only a HEAP-FREE element `T` (scalar or small all-scalar struct, <=3 words) at v1; a heap-owning `T` (`String`, `Vec[_]`, a struct/enum with a heap field) or a WIDE `T` (>3 words) is LOUD-GATED under `karac build` (and default JIT `karac run`) with a `--interp` hint. The interpreter handles all `T` correctly. Three distinct gaps make heap/wide `T` unsafe under codegen today, each needing its own fix: (1) SUCCESS-PATH element leak — `set(v)` moves `v`'s bytes (e.g. a String's {ptr,len,cap} header) into the cell sharing the buffer; the type-erased `karac_runtime_once_free` reclaims only the header+control-block, so the element's inner heap (the char buffer) leaks. A prototype fix (carry `karac_drop_<T>` in `FreeOnceHandle`, run it on `karac_runtime_once_get`'s value ptr before free) made a single-`set` `OnceLock[String]` loop LSan-clean (0 lost, was 490 B/200 iters) — but it does NOT cover gaps 2-3, so it was reverted in favor of the loud gate. (2) REJECTED-VALUE leak — a second `set` on a filled cell returns `Err(AlreadySetError { rejected: v2 })` carrying `v2`'s words; a `match ... { Err(_) => {} }` that discards it leaks `v2`'s heap (190 B/100 iters for `OnceLock[String]` double-set) — the discarded `AlreadySetError[String]` payload isn't dropped (a move-suppression / Result-Err-drop gap; needs confirmation whether it's once-specific or a general `Result[_, <struct-with-heap>]` discard-drop gap). (3) WIDE-PAYLOAD panic — `get() -> Option[ref T]` builds the Option via `build_option_some_via_phis` + `coerce_to_payload_words(v, word_count(T))`; a `T` wider than the seeded 3-word inline payload (a struct with a String field = 4 words, or a 4+-scalar struct) overflows the Option payload area and PANICS at `build_insert_value(...).into_struct_value()` (call_dispatch.rs ~4668). Needs the enum payload-boxing path (box a wide payload behind a pointer, as `try_compile_enum_variant` does) in the once `get` construction. FIX DIRECTION (one dedicated slice): (a) wire `FreeOnceHandle` element-drop for the success path (prototype exists); (b) suppress the source temp's own drop on `set` + drop the discarded `AlreadySetError` heap payload on the Err path; (c) box wide/heap payloads in `get`'s `Option[ref T]` (and the `set` Err's `AlreadySetError` payload) instead of inline words. Then remove the `reject_unsupported_once_elem` gate (src/codegen/once.rs) and add heap-`T` build==run + LSan tests. Scalar + small all-scalar-struct `T` (the config-of-scalars and single-value cases) already work leak-free under build; module-global `OnceLock` of a scalar works too. Not urgent — the interpreter is correct and the gate is loud, not a silent miscompile.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: self-review while extending OnceLock codegen to heap element types (phase-8 lazy-init slice 9 tail) —  INVESTIGATION 2026-07-12 (String/Vec sub-slice attempted, reverted): the three gaps are now precisely characterized. GAP 1 (success-path element leak) is SOLVED by a prototype: `FreeOnceHandle` carries `karac_drop_<T>` (computed at the let-binding via emit_drop_fn_for_type_expr when type_expr_has_drop_heap), and the drain runs it on the `karac_runtime_once_get` value ptr (null-guarded) before `karac_runtime_once_free`. With this, a single-`set` `OnceLock[String]` loop is valgrind-clean (0 lost). GET-BORROW is already handled: `get()` uses method name 'get', so `scrutinee_is_borrow_call` classifies the arm binding as a borrow (no arm-drop) with no change. GAP 3 (wide >3-word `T`) still needs Option payload boxing. GAP 2 (rejected-value leak on double-`set`) is the BLOCKER and is now root-caused: `match c.set(x) { Err(_) => {} }` does NOT free the `AlreadySetError[String]` rejected value (490 B/200 iters). It is NOT a general Result-drop gap — a hand-built `Err(AlreadySetError{rejected: s.to_string()})` discarded via `Err(_)` IS leak-free, and a `Result[i64, MyErr{String}]` discard is leak-free. The cause: the fresh-temp-scrutinee drop (`materialize_freshtemp_enum_scrutinee`, control_flow_match.rs) reads the GENERIC seeded `Result` layout's `field_drop_kinds`, which do NOT reflect a GENERICALLY-INSTANTIATED heap Err payload (`AlreadySetError[String]`); a concrete user Err type (`MyErr`) carries its heap drop-kind in the layout, so it works. Registering the concrete `Result[Unit, AlreadySetError[T]]` at the set-call span in `enum_inst_type_exprs` did NOT fix it (that table isn't the lever for this scrutinee shape). FIX for gap 2 needs the generic-enum-instantiation Err-payload drop: recompute the fresh-temp Result scrutinee's per-variant drop-kinds from the concrete instantiation (or drop the rejected value in `set`'s Err arm without breaking `Err(e) => use(e.rejected)`). Since gap 2 is deep + the two-path ownership of `set`'s value can't be resolved by a local codegen tweak (freeing on the Err arm UAFs the recover-the-value use case), heap-`T` stays loud-gated. `get_or_init` scalar shipped separately (ba537b8). INVESTIGATION 2026-07-12b (deep dive; no code landed — gaps 2+3 need a dedicated slice, gate kept). REPRO REFINEMENT: the heap-`T` leaks reproduce ONLY when the `set` argument is a FRESH TEMP (`cell.set("x".to_string())`); a NAMED-BINDING source (`let m = "x".to_string(); cell.set(m)`) is ACCIDENTALLY leak-free because the source binding's own scope-exit drop frees the buffer while the cell/Err hold un-freed bitwise copies. So any real fix must FLIP ownership to the cell (success) / Err (reject) and MOVE-SUPPRESS the source — otherwise fixing the fresh-temp leak double-frees the named-binding case. GAP 1 (success-path element leak) — SOLVED + VERIFIED (prototype, reverted): (a) move-suppress the `set` arg exactly like `Vec.push` (suppress_source_vec_cleanup_for_arg + the inline Option/Result/boxed-payload + fstr-acc + map-handle siblings, and maybe_defensive_copy_param_arg for an owned String/Vec PARAM source so the caller-retained buffer isn't aliased); (b) add `elem_drop: Option<FunctionValue>` to CleanupAction::FreeOnceHandle, computed at the let-binding via emit_drop_fn_for_type_expr when type_expr_has_drop_heap; the drain runs it on karac_runtime_once_get(handle) (null-guarded, branch) BEFORE karac_runtime_once_free. Verified valgrind-clean on BOTH fresh-temp AND named-binding OnceLock[String] loops (200x, 0 lost, no double-free) — the prototype now also covers the named-binding double-free risk the earlier note missed. GAP 2 (rejected-value leak on double-`set`) — REMAINS THE BLOCKER, root cause SHARPENED and a prior assumption CORRECTED: with gap 1 fixed, a fresh-temp double-`set` still leaks the rejected `Err(AlreadySetError[str]).rejected` (2,400 B/200 for "second-value"). Three drop paths were traced and NONE fire for a struct-wrapping-heap Err: (i) the SEEDED generic `Result` layout (declarations.rs ~4128) has field_drop_kinds = all EnumDropKind::None for Ok AND Err, so track_enum_var / materialize_freshtemp_enum_scrutinee never drop ANY Result payload; (ii) the concrete FreeInlineResultPayload path (track_inline_result_payload_var -> result_inline_payload_elems -> inline_heap_payload_elem, types_lowering.rs ~901) returns None for an Err arm that is a STRUCT wrapping heap (AlreadySetError[str]) — it only recognizes Err = a DIRECT String/Vec; (iii) routing `match cell.set(v)` through a typed let-bound scrutinee `let r: Result[Unit, AlreadySetError[str]] = cell.set(v)` (the B-21/B-23 shared-payload lever, extended to a `.set()` MethodCall gated on the AlreadySetError Err arm) does NOT help — it lands on the same result_inline_payload_elems=None. The B-21/B-23 shared-payload lever does NOT transfer to a struct-wrapping-heap Err. CORRECTION to the earlier note: the claim 'a Result[i64, MyErr{String}] discard is leak-free' and 'a hand-built Err(AlreadySetError{...}) discarded is leak-free' are DCE ARTIFACTS — a discarded PURE Err allocation is optimized away (measured: `match boom(){Ok=>{}Err=>{}}` compiles to `total heap usage: 1 alloc`), so those tests never exercised the drop path. `cell.set()` leaks only because set() is SIDE-EFFECTING and survives DCE. So gap 2 is a GENERAL 'discarded Result whose Ok/Err arm is a struct-wrapping-heap payload is never dropped' gap (once.set is just the case that survives DCE), NOT a once-specific quirk and NOT a generic-instantiation quirk. FIX PLAN for gap 2 (one dedicated slice, broad blast radius — touches every Result binding): extend result_inline_payload_elems + the FreeInlineResultPayload drain (runtime.rs ~5653) to handle an Ok/Err arm that is a heap-BEARING struct/enum by GEP-ing to the payload words and running emit_drop_fn_for_type_expr(payload_te), gated against double-free via the existing suppress_inline_result_payload move-out machinery. Then route once.set through the typed let-bound scrutinee (or drop-track the fresh-temp once-set Result with the concrete te). This likely also subsumes the sibling B-2026-07-12-24 (Result[shared] no scope-exit RC release) — same 'concrete Result payload drop never fires' root. GAP 3 (wide >3-word `T`) UNCHANGED: get()'s Option[ref T] + set()'s Err payload still need the enum payload-boxing path (panic reproduced at call_dispatch.rs ~4860 for a struct-with-String T). NOTE: String and Vec[_] are EXACTLY 3 words (fit), so a future slice could unblock those (heap-but-fitting) after gaps 1+2 while keeping the `wide` gate for struct-with-heap-field T. Gate kept fully in place this session; interp remains correct. INVESTIGATION 2026-07-12c (session 3, post B-24/B-26): with B-2026-07-12-24 (Result[shared] release) landed and B-2026-07-12-26 (AlreadySetError baked-struct field registration) FIXED this session, the remaining B-2 blocker is precisely characterized. DISCARD-PATH FIX — DESIGNED + VALGRIND-VERIFIED (not landed, needs the recovery gap closed first): gaps 1+2 (success-path element leak + rejected-discard leak) are fully solved by: (a) `compile_once_set` move-suppresses its argument via the `Vec.push` suppressor suite (suppress_source_vec_cleanup_for_arg + inline Option/Result/boxed + fstr-acc + map, and maybe_defensive_copy_param_arg for a param source); (b) `CleanupAction::FreeOnceHandle` carries an `elem_drop` fn (emit_drop_fn_for_type_expr) run on `karac_runtime_once_get`(handle) null-guarded before `once_free`; (c) a lowering rewrite routes `match cell.set(v)` through a typed let-bound scrutinee, and `inline_heap_payload_elem` treats the transparent single-field `AlreadySetError[H]` as its inner heap `H`, so the existing `FreeInlineResultPayload` tracker + cap-word move-out suppression free the discarded rejected value. VERIFIED valgrind-clean + interp==AOT on: fresh-temp & named-binding `OnceLock[String]` single-set (2200); double-set discard (1000); `OnceLock[Vec[i64]]` (600). BLOCKER — HEAP-VALUE RECOVERY (`OnceLock[String]` `Err(e) => use e.rejected`): a BROADER pre-existing generic-struct heap-payload recovery gap, NOT once-specific. Two coupled sub-gaps: (i) the Err-arm payload word count for a bare binding comes from the generic struct base (`{i64}` = 1 word), truncating a 3-word String/Vec payload → garbage/leak; a fix attempt (size from the concrete `pattern_binding_inner_types` instantiation via a `generic_struct_binding_word_count` helper) did NOT take effect this session (the inner-type table appears not populated for the Err binding — needs a typechecker-side trace); (ii) even bind-first (`let s = e.rejected; s.len()`), and the direct user analogue `Result[i64, MyErr[String]]` `Err(e) => e.info.len()`, hit the general `__field_elem_0` method-dispatch gap (LOUD for user types). So heap recovery needs the general generic-struct heap-payload-out-of-Result-Err machinery, a separate slice affecting user types. NET: B-2 stays OPEN. Full ungate of heap-`T` `OnceLock` requires the heap-recovery gap above; ungating without it would turn the loud gate into a SILENT miscompile for the recovery pattern. The discard-path fix is ready to re-apply once recovery is sound (or once a per-arm loud gate for heap recovery is added). GAP 3 (wide >3-word `T` payload boxing) unchanged. INVESTIGATION 2026-07-12d (general generic-struct heap-payload recovery — ATTEMPTED end-to-end, WORKS on witnesses, but REVERTED: too high blast-radius, regressed the hot Option[ref T]/String reconstruction path). Verified the correct fix touches SIX layers; a working prototype made both `Result[i64, MyErr[String]]` recovery (`Err(e) => e.info` bind-first AND `e.info.len()` chained) AND the non-generic `PlainErr` baseline all print the right value (12), but the SAME build hung/emptied `test_e2e_vec_get_first_option_ref_t_reread` (Vec.first() -> Option[ref String]) and perturbed Tensor IR-assertion tests (79 codegen failures / hangs). Root: the change fires on the hot generic/borrow payload-binding reconstruction path. The SIX layers (all needed together, each pinpointed): (1) TYPECHECKER `src/typechecker/patterns.rs::record_pattern_inner_type` — add an arm recording the full struct `TypeExpr` for a `Type::Named` generic struct (`!args.is_empty() && env.structs.contains_key(name)`) so codegen can recover the concrete instantiation; (2) `pattern_payload_word_count` (control_flow_match.rs) — size a generic-struct Binding from the concrete mono width via a `generic_struct_binding_word_count` helper (else the `struct_types` `{i64}` base = 1 word truncates a 3-word String); (3) `pattern_payload_llvm_type` — same, return the mono aggregate; (4) `reconstruct_payload_value`'s multi-word struct-build `target_ty` (control_flow_match.rs ~2716) — prefer the mono struct type over the generic base (else 3 words collapse into a 1-field base aggregate); (5) `pattern_binding.rs` payload-bind — register the concrete instantiation into `enum_inst_var_types` so field access resolves generic args (mirror of the let-binding B-2026-07-11-31 registration); (6) `lower_field_access_ptr` (calls.rs) — GEP the field through the mono struct type via a `mono_receiver_struct_type` helper (else the field offset/type is the base's). NARROWING TRIED (insufficient): gating layers 2-4 on `mono_words > base_words` kept the witnesses green but STILL hung vec_get — so at least one of the typechecker record / enum_inst / field-GEP layers perturbs Option[ref T]/String bindings and needs its own borrow-exclusion + width guard. CONCLUSION: this is a genuinely large, test-driven, dedicated codegen slice (must exclude borrow `ref T` payload bindings, must not touch same-width bindings, must be validated against the FULL codegen E2E + memory_sanitizer suite before landing, not just the recovery witnesses). It is the true prerequisite for B-2's heap-`T` ungate (the discard-path fix is ready to layer on top). NOT attempted again without a from-scratch, incremental, per-layer-gated implementation. B-2026-07-12-26 (scalar AlreadySetError field registration) remains the only landed piece. INVESTIGATION 2026-07-12e (the heap-value RECOVERY blocker is FIXED — filed + landed as its own bug B-2026-07-12-27, a6469627): the blocker was a GENERAL, live silent miscompile (NOT once-specific) — moving a heap field out of a concretely-instantiated GENERIC user-struct payload bound from an Option/Result/enum match arm truncated the binding to the all-i64 generic base (3-word String -> 1 word), so `Err(e) => e.rejected` read garbage and `e.rejected.len()` hit the loud __field_elem_0 gap. Fixed across 6 layers (typechecker records the concrete instantiation TypeExpr for the payload binding; codegen recovers mono width + field GEP via enum_inst_var_types), each gated on is_generic_named_struct_type_expr / a special-lowered-builtin exclusion so borrow ref T and Vec/Map/Atomic/... are untouched (the 2026-07-12d 79-test regression avoided). Full codegen E2E 2248/0 + memory_sanitizer 650/0. With B-27 landed, OnceLock[String] `Err(e) => e.rejected` recovery is now SOUND under codegen and ungating no longer risks a SILENT miscompile for the recovery pattern. REMAINING for the heap-T ungate (this bug stays OPEN): (a) re-apply the DISCARD-PATH fix gaps 1+2 (compile_once_set arg move-suppression + FreeOnceHandle elem_drop + route match cell.set through a typed let-bound scrutinee so the discarded AlreadySetError[H] rejected value is freed — designed + valgrind-verified per 2026-07-12c, ready to layer on top of B-27); (b) GAP 3 wide >3-word T payload boxing in get()'s Option[ref T] and set()'s Err payload; then remove/narrow reject_unsupported_once_elem and add heap-T build==run + LSan tests. SEPARATE pre-existing bug found alongside B-27 (fails identically on clean main): returning a generic struct BY VALUE through a match arm fails module verification (ret i64 0 vs the mono struct type) — a distinct generic-struct return-ABI gap, own entry. EMPIRICAL SCOPING 2026-07-12f (post-B-27, correctness foundation confirmed): temporarily ungated the `heap` arm of reject_unsupported_once_elem (once.rs) and rebuilt — ALL OnceLock heap-but-FITTING-T correctness cases now BUILD==RUN vs the interpreter: OnceLock[String] single-set (25), double-set-discard (25), RECOVER `Err(e) => println(e.rejected)` (second) AND `e.rejected.len()` (6), OnceLock[Vec[i64]] (10). So for String/Vec (exactly 3 words, fit the Result/Option inline area) the ONLY remaining blocker is the LEAK (gaps 1+2); CORRECTNESS is fully sound thanks to B-27 (gate reverted after the probe, no code landed). The leak half is the DROP-SIBLING of B-27's recovery fix — a GENERAL gap: a discarded/scope-exited Result/Option whose Ok/Err payload is a heap-bearing struct (incl. a transparent single-field wrapper like AlreadySetError[String]/Wrap[String]) is never dropped — `Result[i64, Wrap[String]] { Err(_) => {} }` leaks the String on main too (needs Linux LSan to see). NEXT SLICE (validatable now — kara-lsan docker image built + colima running; iterate via `scripts/lsan-local.sh <filter>`): (1) FreeOnceHandle carries an elem_drop fn (emit_drop_fn_for_type_expr) run on karac_runtime_once_get(handle) null-guarded before once_free, + move-suppress compile_once_set's arg (Vec.push suppressor suite) so the source doesn't double-free; (2) extend inline_heap_payload_elem / result_inline_payload_elems + the FreeInlineResultPayload drain to treat a TRANSPARENT single-field heap-wrapping struct (AlreadySetError[H], Wrap[H]) as its inner heap H, double-free-gated via the existing consuming-arm suppressor (suppress_inline_result_payload_cleanup) so `Err(e) => use e.rejected` recovery does NOT double-free; then ungate heap-but-fitting T (KEEP the `wide` gate for struct-with-heap-field T = gap 3), add build==run + LSan tests. GAP 3 (wide >3-word T payload boxing in get()'s Option[ref T] + set()'s Err payload) unchanged. INVESTIGATION 2026-07-13b (heap-`T` OnceLock UNGATE attempted end-to-end, REVERTED — success+recovery valgrind-clean, but the DISCARD drop can't be closed without either an ownership-checker regression or delicate match-compilation surgery; gate kept). Built the full ungate on top of the landed recovery slice and VALGRIND-VERIFIED (AOT, Linux) leak-free + double-free-free on: single-set fresh-temp & named-binding `OnceLock[String]` (4/4 allocs-frees); 100x named loop (301/301); `OnceLock[Vec[i64]]` set+reject-recover+get (0 errors); recovery-READ (`Err(e)=>e.rejected.len()`) and recovery-MOVE-OUT (`Err(e)=>println(e.rejected)`) both 0 leaks/0 UAF; wide struct-with-heap `T` still correctly loud-gated. PIECES THAT WORKED (all reverted, all sound in isolation): (1) `FreeOnceHandle` carries an `elem_drop` fn run on `karac_runtime_once_get(handle)` (null-guarded) before `once_free` — frees the sealed value's inner heap [gap 1 success leak]; (2) `compile_once_set` move-suppresses its arg via `suppress_source_vec_cleanup_for_arg` — the cell/Err owns the buffer, source doesn't double-free; (3) `reject_unsupported_once_elem` loosened to gate only `wide`(>3w) + `get_or_init`-heap, allowing 3-word String/Vec through set/get; (4) the RECOVERY binding gets a TRANSPARENT-WRAPPER drop — `AlreadySetError[H]` is a single-field `{rejected:H}` wrapper (field at offset 0), so `emit_drop_fn_for_type_expr(H)` on the binding's alloca frees exactly the inner heap; a move-out of `e.rejected` zeros that field's cap so the drop skips it [this cleanly solved the two-path recovery ownership the prior notes called THE blocker — a new `transparent_wrapper_heap_field_te` helper]; (5) `inline_heap_payload_elem` treats `AlreadySetError[H]` as inner heap `H`. THE REMAINING BLOCKER — DISCARD DROP (`match cell.set(v){Err(_)=>{}}` on an already-set cell leaks the rejected value, 1200B/100 in the repro): the fresh-temp `Result` scrutinee's drop is resolved from the ERASED seeded `Result` layout (all-`None` drop-kinds), so no concrete drop fires. The coordination that WOULD fix it already exists but only for a NAMED scrutinee: `track_inline_result_payload_var` + `suppress_inline_result_payload_cleanup` (zeros the payload `cap` at Result-field 3 when an arm BINDS the payload, so the binding's own drop is sole owner; an `Err(_)` discard leaves it armed and the scrutinee frees). TWO ROADS, both blocked here: (A) LOWERING REWRITE — rewrite `match cell.set(v)` -> `{let r=cell.set(v); match r{..}}` (the B-21/B-23 lever, extended to a `.set` MethodCall gated on `Result[_,AlreadySetError[_]]`). This makes discard leak-clean BUT introduces a bogus OWNERSHIP-CHECKER error: the block-wrapped synthetic binding makes the ownership checker treat the second `cell.set(...)` as MOVING `cell`, so a later `cell.get()` fails `karac check` ('value cell moved here, used again'). Reproduces even for SCALAR `OnceLock[i64]` recovery+get (which shipped fine pre-rewrite), so the rewrite is a regression, not viable as-is — the ownership checker mishandles the synthetic Block+method-receiver+move-out combination. (B) CODEGEN-LEVEL MATERIALIZE (no AST rewrite, no ownership impact) — in `materialize_freshtemp_enum_scrutinee`, for a fresh-temp `cell.set()` `Result` scrutinee build the concrete `Result[Unit,AlreadySetError[elem]]` te (elem from `once_var_types`), materialize into a synthetic alloca, register it in `inline_result_payload_vars` + `track_inline_result_payload_var`, and thread an inline-result payload suppression through the match-arm bind so a bound `Err(e)` zeros the synthetic's cap (leaving e's transparent-wrapper drop the sole owner) while an `Err(_)` discard frees via the synthetic. This is the CORRECT path but is substantial match-compilation surgery with real double-free risk (needs the full valgrind matrix: discard / recover-read / recover-move-out / vec / named-vs-fresh-temp), so deferred rather than rushed. NET: B-2 stays OPEN. The recovery slice (2f3071d) is landed; the ungate is one dedicated codegen slice away (road B), with roads (1)-(5) above proven valgrind-clean and ready to re-apply once the discard coordination lands. DO NOT ship the ungate with road (A) — it regresses `karac check`. SLICE LANDED 2026-07-12g (heap-FITTING T ungated leak-free, c0563be9): gaps 1+2 closed. OnceLock/OnceCell now support a heap-owning but FITTING element T (String, Vec, or a single-heap-field wrapper struct, <=3 words) under karac build, leak-free + double-free-free (full memory_sanitizer suite 659/0 under Linux LSan, incl. 7 new asan_oncelock_* tests: set_get, double_set discard, reject_recover read+consume, single-field struct, vec; codegen E2E 2248/0). GAP 1 (success-path element leak): CleanupAction::FreeOnceHandle carries an elem_drop fn (emit_drop_fn_for_type_expr) run on karac_runtime_once_get(handle) null-guarded before once_free; compile_once_set move-suppresses its arg (suppress_source_vec_cleanup_for_arg) so the source doesn't double-free the buffer the cell owns. GAP 2 (rejected-value leak): a fresh-temp `match cell.set(v) {...}` scrutinee registers FreeInlineResultPayload via the new track_freshtemp_inline_result_scrutinee (the seeded Result all-None drop-kinds meant materialize_freshtemp_enum_scrutinee never tracked it), with inline_heap_payload_elem extended to see a TRANSPARENT single-field wrapper (AlreadySetError[H]/Wrap[H]) as its inner heap H; discard Err(_) frees it, consuming Err(e)=>move suppresses (consumer owns), and a borrow-only recover-READ (Err(e)=>e.rejected.len()) on a STRUCT-WRAPPER skips the suppress so the source frees at arm-end — gated on struct-wrapper (a direct String/Vec payload keeps its own track_vec_var and always suppresses, else double-free). BLAST-RADIUS AVOIDED: an earlier cut made emit_struct_drop_synthesis_mono subst bare-generic-param fields to give the bound e a mono struct-drop — REVERTED because Box[String] (already freed elsewhere) then double-freed (exit 133, caught by test_e2e_generic_narrow_int_method_multi_instantiation); the borrow-only-struct-wrapper approach needs no synth_drop change. The `wide` (>3-word) gate is KEPT. Gate test updated: test_oncelock_wide_element_gated_heap_fitting_ok (was ..._rejected). REMAINING: GAP 3 (wide >3-word T payload boxing in get()'s Option[ref T] + set()'s Err payload) — still loud-gated. B-2 stays OPEN until gap 3. NOTE ordering: the 2026-07-12g slice (LANDED, c0563be9) SUPERSEDES the concurrent 2026-07-13b investigation's 'reverted, gate kept' conclusion — 13b reverted the discard drop as too-delicate match surgery; 12g CLOSED it via track_freshtemp_inline_result_scrutinee (fresh-temp inline-Result match-scrutinee FreeInlineResultPayload) + the struct-wrapper-gated borrow-only recover-READ skip, LSan-verified 659/0. GAP 3 SCOPE (the sole remaining piece; wide >3-word T): once get() loads the full T and coerce_to_payload_words(word_count(T)) then build_option_some_via_phis, which PANICS when word_count > the Option inline area (3). Boxing lever EXISTS: pass num_words=area so coerce_to_payload_words boxes (out.len()>area -> malloc+store+ptr-in-word0), reconstruct_payload_value deboxes (want>field_words.len()). BUT the ownership is the hard part: (a) an ALL-SCALAR wide T ({i64x4}) boxes a copy cleanly; (b) a wide-WITH-HEAP T ({i64,String}) boxes a COPY whose heap-field ptrs ALIAS the cell's own heap -> double-free with FreeOnceHandle's elem_drop; get() returns Option[ref T] so the box should arguably hold a BORROW (ptr to the sealed T, 1 word) rather than a boxed value-copy. set()'s Err(AlreadySetError[wide T]) payload needs the same boxing (mirror try_compile_enum_variant). A distinct feature-slice with a representation decision (by-ref vs boxed value-copy); the `wide` gate stays LOUD + graceful (--interp fallback) until then. B-2 stays OPEN on gap 3. RESOLVED 2026-07-13 (c4d61cb, gap 3 — the last remaining piece; gaps 1+2 landed c0563be9): `OnceLock`/`OnceCell` `set`/`get` now support a WIDE element `T` (> 3 words) under `karac build`; the `reject_unsupported_once_elem` gate is removed. THREE sub-fixes, all landed + validated (codegen E2E 2271/0, memory_sanitizer LSan 669/0, clippy + fmt clean): (A) GET PAYLOAD BOXING — `compile_once_get` passed `num_words = word_count(T)` to `build_option_some_via_phis`, which inserts one word per element into the FIXED 3-word `Option` area and PANICKED past field 3. Cap at the Option area (`.clamp(1, 3)`) so `coerce_to_payload_words` heap-BOXES a wide value (shallow bit-copy behind a pointer) — the `Vec.get`/`Map.get` `Option[ref T]` convention; the borrow scrutinee's `track_freshtemp_boxed_enum_scrutinee` box-ONLY free leaves the cell's `FreeOnceHandle` elem-drop sole owner of the interior heap (no leak, no double-free). `set`'s `Err(AlreadySetError{rejected})` payload already boxes past the 5-word `Result` area via `build_nonshared_enum_value`. (B) CHAINED GENERIC-MONO FIELD READ (general, NOT once-specific — reproduces on plain `Result[_, Wrap[Rec]]`) — a chained field READ through a generic-instantiated struct payload binding (`Err(e) => e.inner.a` where `e: Wrap[Pair]`) silently read the `i64 0` placeholder: `type_name_of_expr`'s FieldAccess arm returned the generic PARAM name (`T`) for `e.inner` from `struct_field_type_names`, so `field_index_for` found no field layout. Now substitutes the struct's generic params with the concrete args from `enum_inst_var_types` (self-gated to a recorded generic instantiation via `is_generic_named_struct_type_expr`, so non-generic receivers are untouched — no blast radius). This is the READ-side sibling of the B-27 recovery fix, which covered a DIRECT 3-word inner (`Wrap[String]`) but not a nested multi-field struct inner. (C) DISCARDED STRUCT-WITH-HEAP RESULT PAYLOAD DROP (general) — a discarded fresh-temp `Result` whose Ok/Err payload is a multi-field struct-with-heap (or a transparent wrapper of one like `AlreadySetError[Rec]`) leaked its inner heap (`match cell.set(v){Err(_)=>{}}` on a wide struct element = 6 B/iter). The seeded `Result` layout carries no drop kind, and the `{ptr,len,cap}` overlay only frees a single buffer at payload offset 0 (`inline_heap_payload_elem` returns None for a multi-field struct). `FreeInlineResultPayload` gains a struct-drop arm (`result_inline_payload_struct_drops`/`inline_struct_payload_drop` compute `karac_drop_<payload>` — peeling a transparent wrapper to its concrete inner so a generic `AlreadySetError[Rec]` drops via `Rec`; the drain runs it on a pointer to the payload area). A consuming move-out arm now zeros the WHOLE payload area (`suppress_inline_result_payload_cleanup*` → `zero_result_payload_area`) so BOTH the overlay `cap`-guard AND the struct drop's per-field cap-guards skip — the existing `arm_only_borrows` + `struct_wrapper` skip logic keeps a borrow-only read freed by the drain (no leak, no double-free). Wired through all three Result inline-payload trackers (fresh-temp scrutinee, named binding, discarded statement). GET_OR_INIT still gates a NON-SCALAR `T` (its closure returns an aggregate via the deferred sret ABI) — a separate slice, orthogonal to gap 3. The interpreter was always correct; the gate was loud, so this was never a silent miscompile in shipped binaries. B-2 CLOSED. FOLLOW-ON 2026-07-13 (71e6f96, get_or_init aggregate): `get_or_init(|| ...)` now accepts a heap-FREE aggregate element `T` (a struct/tuple of scalars), not just a scalar — the closure's DIRECT aggregate return (closures use a direct struct return, NOT sret as the earlier note assumed) is now inferred via a new `infer_closure_return_type` StructLiteral arm (previously a `|| S {..}` closure was declared `-> i64` and its struct `ret` tripped the LLVM verifier — a general closure fix, benefits every struct-returning closure). The old gate rejected ALL non-scalar `T`; it now gates only a HEAP-OWNING `T` (`type_expr_has_drop_heap`): get_or_init returns a `ref T` VALUE-COPY that aliases the cell's sealed heap and the caller's binding drops it — an ASAN-verified double-free against the cell's element drop. Unblocking heap-owning `T` needs a by-POINTER borrow representation for get_or_init's `ref T` result (so the caller doesn't drop the aliased value) — a deferred follow-on; `set`/`get` are unaffected (they don't return a caller-owned value-copy). Tests: get_or_init heap-free-aggregate build==interp + LSan loop + closure-returns-struct-literal E2E; both get_or_init gate tests updated. FOLLOWUP CLOSED (407be19, 2026-07-21): the deferred heap-owning-T get_or_init landed — not via the sketched by-pointer borrow, but as a BORROWED cap-0 VIEW of the loaded value-copy (zero_heap_caps_in_value, recursive; the Interner/Arena {ptr,len,cap=0} convention), so the caller's cap-guarded frees no-op and the cell's elem-drop stays single owner. Racing-set loser's value freed in place (goi.set.lost arm). let-site binding registration via register_var_from_type_expr for method dispatch on the result. LSan-clean (3 asan_oncelock_get_or_init_* twins), interp==JIT==AOT, both former gate assertions flipped. OnceLock/OnceCell surface now COMPLETE for any T.

title: Assignment through a `mut ref Option[shared]` parameter does not write back to the caller on codegen (interpreter correct) — silent wrong result, SIGSEGV downstream
detail: A function parameter of type `mut ref Option[shared T]` that the callee REASSIGNS (`param = Some(n)` / `param = other_opt`) does not propagate the store back to the caller's binding under codegen (JIT + AOT); the interpreter is correct. The write appears to land in a callee-local copy of the Option rather than through the by-ref pointer to the caller's slot, so on return the caller still sees the pre-call value. Manifests as a silent wrong answer, and SIGSEGVs downstream when the stale state (e.g. a None that should be Some) is later unwrapped and its tag/word is used as a pointer (`Invalid read of size 8, Address 0x1`). MINIMAL REPRO (interp prints `prev val: 3`; codegen JIT+AOT print `prev none`): `shared struct Node { mut val: i64, mut next: Option[Node] } fn walk(node: Option[Node], prev: mut ref Option[Node]) { match node { None => {} Some(n) => { walk(n.next, prev); prev = Some(n); } } } fn main() { let a = Some(Node { val: 3, next: Some(Node { val: 5, next: None }) }); let mut prev: Option[Node] = None; walk(a, mut prev); match prev { None => { println("prev none"); } Some(p) => { println(f"prev val: {p.val}"); } } }`. The recursion is not required to trigger it — a single-level `mut ref Option[shared]` reassign reproduces the lost writeback; recursion just makes the stale state fatal (kata #99 recover-BST recursive-inorder solver, which threads `prev`/`first`/`second` as `mut ref Option[TreeNode]`, SIGSEGVs on codegen while the interpreter recovers correctly). Non-shared `mut ref Option[T]` (scalar/String payload) and `mut ref T` for a bare shared `T` are NOT known to be affected — the gap is specifically the Option[shared] overlay assignment (which does an rc-dec of the old inner + rc-inc of the new) storing to the wrong (local) slot instead of through the ref pointer. FIX AREA: the Assign arm's Identifier-target path (src/codegen/stmts.rs) must, when the target names a `mut ref Option[shared]` parameter (in `ref_params`), resolve the store destination to the by-ref pointer and run the Option[shared] retain/release store THROUGH it, rather than into the param's local alloca. WORKAROUND: keep the threaded state as LOCAL bindings (iterative explicit-stack or a returned tuple) instead of `mut ref Option[shared]` params; the #99 kata ships the ★ adjacent-descent scan + a sort-based recover, both of which keep Option[shared] state local and are byte-identical across interp/JIT/AOT/auto-par.
source: kata:leetcode-99-recover-bst (recover_recursive.kara, recursive inorder threading mut ref Option[TreeNode] state)

title: Pushing a FIELD-READ `Option[shared]` (`stack.push(n.left)`) onto a `Vec[Option[shared]]` and dropping the Vec with residual elements LEAKS the pushed handles; a fresh `Some(..)` push is clean
detail: A `Vec[Option[shared T]]` used as a worklist/stack that has a shared handle pushed via a FIELD READ of another shared node (`stack.push(n.left)` / `stack.push(n.right)`) leaks that handle if the Vec is dropped at scope exit while still holding it (LSan/valgrind: `definitely lost`). The retain applied when the field-read Option[shared] is pushed is not balanced by a release on the Vec's scope-exit element-drop for that residual element: net rc ends at 1 and the node (plus its subtree) leaks. DISCRIMINATOR: pushing a FRESH `Some(Node{..})` element into the same `Vec[Option[shared]]` and dropping it with residuals is CLEAN (the fresh value owns its +1 and the Vec drop releases it) — the leak is specific to the field-read (aliasing) push. Not a use-after-free / double-free (values are correct); a pure leak. MINIMAL REPRO (32-byte leak): `shared struct Node { val: i64, mut left: Option[Node], mut right: Option[Node] } fn main() { let root = Some(Node { val: 1, left: Some(Node { val: 2, left: None, right: None }), right: None }); let mut stack: Vec[Option[Node]] = Vec.new(); match root { None => {} Some(n) => { stack.push(n.left); } } println(f"len: {stack.len()}"); }`. CLEAN COUNTERPART (no leak): the same program pushing `Some(Node { val: 2, left: None, right: None })` (a fresh literal) instead of `n.left`. FIX AREA: either the field-read Option[shared] push must NOT retain (matching the fresh-push convention where the Vec drop is the sole releaser) OR the Vec[Option[shared]] scope-exit element-drop must release residual field-pushed elements symmetrically with the retain — the push retain (B-2026-07-11-29 push co-ownership inc, which fires for Identifier/consuming-call args) and the residual-drop release must agree for the FieldAccess push shape. Same systemically-under-supported Vec-of-Option[shared] RC surface as B-2026-07-11-29. WORKAROUND: drain the worklist fully before returning (no residual elements), or use a non-worklist formulation. Surfaced by kata #100 (same-tree): the iterative paired-stack solver (two `Vec[Option[TreeNode]]` worklists, `stack.push(pn.left)` etc.) leaks on every early `false` return that leaves node-pairs on the stacks; the recursive lockstep solver and the serialize-and-compare solver (both shipped) are byte-identical across all surfaces and valgrind-clean. INVESTIGATION 2026-07-12: the current manifestation on main is a USE-AFTER-FREE, not a leak — valgrind on the minimal residual repro shows `Invalid read of size 8` on a freed 32-byte Node (the residual field-pushed element is under-retained: the Vec's per-element RcDecOption AND the parent field's drop both dec it → freed while still referenced). A naive fix (retain the field-read Option[shared] on push, e.g. a value-based `retain_option_shared_value` for the FieldAccess arg) FIXES the residual UAF but REGRESSES the previously-clean drain-and-consume case into a 64-byte LEAK: the residual path decs the element via the Vec scope-exit drop, but the pop()+nested-match consume path does NOT dec the inner shared handle, so the two consumption paths are COUPLED and disagree on rc — balancing one unbalances the other. A sound fix must make pop()+consume and the residual Vec-drop agree (both dec exactly once per push-retain) before adding the push-side retain; they cannot be fixed independently. Same coupled Vec-of-Option[shared] surface as B-2026-07-11-29. INVESTIGATION 2026-07-12 (b): DECOMPOSED into TWO coupled defects with a full four-case truth table (push shape {fresh Some(..) | field-read n.left} x fate {residual: left on stack, Vec dropped | drain: pop()+consume all}); each case built + valgrind'd on main HEAD. DEFECT 2 (push-side): `stack.push(n.left)` — a FieldAccess Option[shared] arg — does NOT retain. The AoS Vec push arm (vec_method.rs ~1990) calls only `share_option_shared_ref_for_arg` (the Identifier retain, which the comment says covers `out.push(orig)`); it never calls the existing `share_option_shared_field_ref_for_arg` (the FieldAccess companion at call_dispatch.rs ~4389, already used by the generic user-method-call path). So a field-pushed element stays rc=1 while co-owned by BOTH the parent field and the Vec slot. DEFECT 1 (pop-side, INDEPENDENT — affects FRESH pushes too): `pop()` moves the element out (len-- excludes it from the Vec residual drop) but does NOT transfer the moved-out dec obligation to its result. Confirmed: `stack.push(Some(Node{..}))` then drain via `let _ = stack.pop()` (pop-discard), `let x = stack.pop(); match x {..}` (pop-bound), or `match stack.pop() {..}` (fresh-drain) ALL leak 32 B — pop's raw-phi-built `Option[Option[shared]]` result carries no nested RcDecOption. Contrast: a fresh `let x: Option[Option[Node]] = Some(Some(Node{..})); match x {..}` is CLEAN — the general nested-Option[shared] drop-walk works for a CONSTRUCTION-expr binding; only pop()'s result is untracked. The single-Option `track_rc_option_var` / `shared_option_info` path keys on Option[shared] (immediate inner is a heap shared); the pop result's immediate inner is another Option, so that path doesn't fire, and nothing registers the nested dec for the pop result. TRUTH TABLE on main HEAD: fresh+residual CLEAN (rc1, Vec-drop decs); fresh+drain LEAK (rc1, pop transfers no dec); field+residual UAF (rc1, Vec-drop dec + parent-field dec = 2 decs -> Invalid read of size 8); field+drain CLEAN (rc1, no-pop-dec + parent dec = 1). So field+drain is 'accidentally clean' — DEFECT 1 (no pop dec) and DEFECT 2 (no field retain) exactly CANCEL.
