FIX (deferred): when the pushed arg statically types as a BARE `shared struct` AND is an aliasing read (indexed element / field / live binding, not a fresh `Node{..}` / call move-out), emit `emit_refcount_inc` so the container holds an independent +1 — the bare-shared sibling of the Option[shared] push retain.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: kata #133 (Clone Graph), re-verification. Class use-after-free (missing RC retain on push of an aliased bare shared-struct element; source-Vec drop frees the still-referenced node). HIGH severity: silent run-vs-build divergence with heap corruption on the canonical adjacency-list build (`node.neighbors.push(other_node)` where nodes live in a pool Vec). Pre-existing (kata interp-only from creation); NOT a regression from the weak-ref work (bg3 reduction proves the indexed read path correct). Repro reductions: rc.kara (fails: source dropped) vs bg3.kara (clean: source alive). FIXED a4a66c5: added `share_shared_struct_ref_for_arg` (bare-shared sibling of the share_option_shared_* push-retain family) — retains an aliasing Index/FieldAccess bare shared-struct push arg via emit_refcount_inc. Kata #133 bfs+dfs now run==build==JIT==Python; retain is balanced (acyclic LSan-clean; cyclic residual is the inherent RC-cycle leak, out of scope). Regression: tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_push_aliased_bare_shared_element_retained_no_uaf; full memory_sanitizer 819 green.

title: match on a Result[String, i64] FIELD through a `ref` param with a consuming Ok arm double-frees under AOT (O0 and O2): `match h.res { Ok(s) => return "ok:".to_string() + s, Err(e) => … }` (h: ref Holder) aborts free(): double free; interp AND JIT print ok:rr (JIT survives by layout luck). The Result-LEAF sibling of B-2026-07-21-9 — exactly the open territory that fix's resolution flagged: the clone dispatcher has no Result value deep-clone, and the ref-chain clone legs gate Result out, so the inline String payload binding aliases the caller's buffer and both free it.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: probe pass 5 (2026-07-21) — the follow-up sweep the B-2026-07-21-9 resolution called for. Sibling probes CLEAN in the same pass: mut-ref write-back + consuming enum match (r2), struct-pattern Vec-field escape through ref (r3). FIX DIRECTION: mirror the -9 Option leg — a `clone_escaping_borrowed_ref_chain_result` gated on `result_inline_payload_elems` (inline String/Vec halves only; shared/boxed halves keep status quo), a Result-scoped in-place deep copy of the tag-matched half (the `deep_copy_option_inline_payload_in_place` word dance at Result offsets, via load_enum_word/store_enum_word + emit_vecstr_defensive_copy), tracking via `track_inline_result_payload_var` on the clone slot, and the consuming-arm disarm via the existing `suppress_inline_result_payload_cleanup_at`. Medium: Result-typed struct fields consumed through borrows are a plausible parser/self-host shape.
resolution: Fixed (3ea6b06). Implemented exactly as filed — the Result-leaf mirror of the -9 Option leg. (1) `deep_copy_result_inline_heap_halves_in_place` (param_own.rs): tag-guarded in-place deep copy of the LIVE half's `{ptr,len,cap}` overlay at Result words 1..3 (Ok and Err overlay the same words; two guards, each emitted only for a DIRECT inline-heap String/Vec half) via emit_vecstr_defensive_copy. (2) `clone_escaping_borrowed_ref_chain_result` (control_flow_match.rs): same strict FieldAccess-chain / signature_ref_params / escape gates as the siblings; per-half shape gate = direct String/Vec (non-shared element) or heap-free scalar — shared / struct-wrapper / nested-seeded halves keep status quo; clone slot registered via track_inline_result_payload_var, consuming Ok/Err arms disarm it with the existing suppress_inline_result_payload_cleanup_at (match / if-let / let-else routes all wired). ONE IMPLEMENTATION TRAP hit and fixed: the tracker must be called BEFORE the deep copy emits IR — its nested-block defensive zero-init keys on current-block != entry, and the copy's tag-guard blocks made that test fire spuriously, landing a zeroinitializer store in the ENTRY block between the value store and the tag read (clone wiped at runtime: 'err:0' + caller-payload leak on every backend). Verified: 10-shape battery (consuming Ok via return-concat x2 calls, Err-live holder, Result[String,String] both-halves-heap both-tags, borrow-only read, Ok(_) wildcard + scalar-Err consume, if-let, let-else + miss edge, Result[Vec[i64],i64] Ok(v) returned, two-hop o.inner.res chain, mut-ref receiver) — interp/JIT/AOT-O2/AOT-O0 parity + valgrind clean (with drain epilogues; see B-2026-07-21-15 for why they are needed). E2E test_e2e_ref_param_result_field_consume + LSan/ASAN asan_ref_param_result_field_consume_no_leak_no_double_free added. During verification the pre-existing struct-field Result payload scope-exit LEAK (independent of any match/borrow — `field_copy_supported`'s deliberate caller-retains exclusion of Result fields) was isolated and filed as B-2026-07-21-15.

title: a struct with a `Result[String, i64]`-class field leaks the live half's heap payload at the owning struct's scope-exit drop — `let a = Holder { res: Ok("rr".to_string()), n: 1 }; println(a.n.to_string());` leaks the 2-byte "rr" buffer (valgrind, AOT O0/O2). No match, no borrow, no call needed: the struct drop simply never frees a Result field's payload. The Option sibling is CLEAN (`Option[String]` field frees via the struct drop's OptionInline arm).
detail: SOURCE NOTE: isolated during B-2026-07-21-14 verification (probe pass 5 follow-up) — every ref-chain battery shape showed one caller-side leaked block sized exactly like the holder's payload, and the no-consume control (c1: construct + print a scalar field only) reproduces it, proving independence from the match/borrow machinery. ROOT CAUSE (by design, now promoted to a tracked gap): `field_copy_supported` (param_own.rs) deliberately returns false for EVERY `Result` field — 'stay caller-retains (this routine can't duplicate them, and the drop correspondingly leaves them excluded)' — so `aggregate_param_copy_supported_struct` marks the owning struct non-copy-supported, and `emit_struct_drop_synthesis`'s OptionInline pass (gated on that same predicate, and keyed to Some("Option") field names only) never classifies the field; it stays FieldDrop::None and the payload leaks wholesale at every scope-exit drop. The leak-vs-double-free tradeoff was the right call before an entry-copy existed; the -14 fix built the missing copy half. FIX DIRECTION (copy == drop, both sides together): (a) admit the narrow direct-String/Vec-halves Result class in `field_copy_supported` (mirroring the -14 clone leg's per-half gate), (b) entry-copy it in `deep_copy_one_aggregate_field` via the new `deep_copy_result_inline_heap_halves_in_place`, (c) extend the OptionInline classifier pass to Some("Result") fields (the drop fn already exists — `vec_elem_agg_drop_for_type_expr` routes Result to `emit_result_drop_fn`, proven on Vec elements), and (d) AUDIT every owned-struct field-move site (`zero_struct_field_move_cap` and the destructure suppressions) for a Result arm — flipping these structs to callee-owned without the move-site zeroing turns today's leak into tomorrow's double-free on `match holder.res { Ok(s) => return s }` over an OWNED local. That audit is why this is its own slice and was NOT folded into -14. WORKAROUND meanwhile: move the field out before scope exit (`let drained = holder.res;` — the binding's FreeInlineResultPayload frees it); the -14 tests use exactly this drain epilogue.
resolution: Fixed (ecf05ca). Completed the copy == drop pair for the DIRECT String/Vec-halves Result class (the -14 predicate `result_field_direct_vecstr_halves_ok` gates every leg, so wider shapes — shared/wrapper/nested halves — stay caller-retains exactly as before): (a) `field_copy_supported` admits the class (false under for-loop strict-shared mode, matching the Option arm); (b) the by-value entry copy duplicates the live half via the -14 `deep_copy_result_inline_heap_halves_in_place` (`deep_copy_one_aggregate_field` Result arm); (c) `emit_struct_drop_synthesis`'s OptionInline classifier pass now also admits Some("Result") fields, registering the existing tag-dispatched `karac_drop_Result_<ok>_<err>` (`vec_elem_agg_drop_for_type_expr` — proven on Vec elements) as the field's drop; (d) every whole/field move site zeroes the source payload area (`zero_result_payload_area`, now pub(super)): `zero_struct_move_caps` (whole-struct move) + `zero_struct_field_move_cap` (arg/field moves) + the #16 struct-pattern destructure arm lose their `fname != "Result"` exclusions, the let-destructure `transferable` gate admits the class with `track_owned_destructure_field_cleanup` registering the leaf's FreeInlineResultPayload, and the direct match/if-let/let-else + plain let/assign moves were already covered by B-2026-07-21-16's legs (built as groundwork). Verified: c1 (the filed no-consume repro) now valgrind-clean; battery of by-value passing (entry copy + caller retains), consuming match on a callee copy, fn-returned holder, struct move + consume, Vec[Holder] element, struct-pattern destructure with consumed AND unconsumed leaves, Err-scalar and Result[String,String] both-halves-heap holders — interp/JIT/AOT-O2/AOT-O0 parity + valgrind clean; the full -14 ref-chain and -16 owned-place batteries re-run green against the now-armed struct drop. Suites: codegen 2537/0, memory_sanitizer 829/0 (Linux LSan), interpreter 1287/0, selfhost_codegen (--features llvm) 1/0, clippy --all-targets clean, fmt clean. E2E test_e2e_struct_result_field_drop_and_moves + LSan/ASAN asan_struct_result_field_drop_no_leak_no_double_free added (single-use holders — the harness ownership gate rejects double-consume reuse; the reuse angle lives in the scratch battery). The -14 tests' drain epilogues remain valid (the -16 let leg makes them true moves).

title: match/if-let/let-else DIRECTLY over an OWNED struct's `Option[String]` field with a payload binding double-frees under AOT — `match a.opt { Some(s) => println(s), None => {} }` (a an owned local `H2 { opt: Option[String], n: i64 }`) frees the payload once via the binding's arm-exit cleanup and AGAIN via the struct drop's OptionInline arm (valgrind Invalid free; output correct — frees run at scope exit). Even a PRINT-ONLY binding arm reproduces. Same class: `let x = a.opt;` or `x = a.opt;` (assign) followed by consuming x. The Result sibling shapes are currently clean only because the struct drop skips Result fields entirely (B-2026-07-21-15's leak).
detail: SOURCE NOTE: isolated during the B-2026-07-21-15 fix-direction audit (the owned-struct move-site sweep the -15 filing called for) — probing what the Option machinery does on the shapes Result would inherit. Verified PRE-EXISTING (a733c33, 2026-07-18, reproduces) — not from the 2026-07-21 ref-chain work, whose clone legs gate on signature ref params and never fire for owned locals. ROOT CAUSE: B-2026-07-03-28 Facet A armed the struct drop's OptionInline free and zeroed the source tag at SOME move sites (#16 struct-pattern destructure, whole-struct move via zero_struct_move_caps, arg/field moves via zero_struct_field_move_cap) — but NOT at (1) a DIRECT variant-pattern match/if-let/let-else scrutinee over the field place (`match a.opt { Some(s) => … }` — the #15 route hands `Option` to the generic enum suppressor, which no-ops on the seeded all-None drop-kind layout), (2) the plain `let x = a.opt;` field move (the Let path registers ownership only for FRESH RHS shapes — Call / rhs_is_fresh_inline_enum — so the move neither zeroes the source nor arms the binding; balanced while unconsumed, double-free once x is consumed), and (3) the `x = a.opt;` assign move (same). Any binding sub-pattern counts — bindings on an owned-place Option-field scrutinee always register an owned free (print-only o6 reproduces). REPRO SET (scratchpad/b15): o1 consume-via-concat, o6 print-only, o5 if-let + let-else, o2/o4 let+consume vs bare-let-balanced, a1 assign+consume, o3 assign-out-of-arm; ro1 Result control clean (drop skips). FIX DIRECTION: (a) a consuming-variant-pattern place-source suppressor at the three pattern sites — pattern gates first (Some/Ok/Err with a binding sub-pattern), leaf `Option`/`Result` via place_chain_type_name, source ptr via field_chain_place_ptr (bails on borrowed roots — the ref-param case is the -9/-14 clone legs' territory), zero the Option TAG / Result payload area; (b) a let/assign move leg beside the #27 enum sibling (suppress_place_field_enum_move_source): zero the source and register the binding's own FreeInline{Option,Result}Payload so a later consume routes through the proven identifier-keyed suppressors. Result arms included from day one: harmless while the struct drop skips Result fields, load-bearing the moment B-2026-07-21-15 arms it. HIGH: memory corruption (abort under hardened allocators) on a completely mundane shape — read an Option[String] field of a local struct with a match.
resolution: Fixed (f9be2c7). Implemented as filed. (1) `place_optres_field_move_info` (control_flow_match.rs): shared resolver for the owned-place field-move legs — FieldAccess place, Option leaf with an inline non-shared String/Vec payload or Result leaf in the direct-String/Vec-halves class (`result_field_direct_vecstr_halves_ok`, extracted from the -14 clone leg so the two stay in lockstep), source pointer via `field_chain_place_ptr` (bails on borrowed roots — the -9/-14 ref-param clone legs' territory — and non-place roots). (2) Pattern leg `suppress_consumed_place_optres_field_source`: in the binding arm, zero the SOURCE (Option tag to None / Result payload area) so the owning struct's drop skips the payload the binding now owns; gated on the scrutinee's bindings actually owning their frees (!pattern_binding_is_borrow, captured while the classification is active — at the if-let/let-else sites the flag is restored before the suppressor cluster, so it is snapshot beforehand). Wired into compile_match / compile_if_let / compile_let_else beside the #15 suppressor. (3) Let leg `track_place_optres_field_move`: `let x = a.opt` now registers x's own FreeInline{Option,Result}Payload AND zeroes the source — the move is real, and a later consuming match routes through the proven identifier-keyed suppressors; also gives the -14 tests' `let dr = h.res` drain route a genuine cleanup registration. (4) Assign leg `suppress_place_optres_field_move_source`: source zero only (the target's let-site cleanup owns the moved-in payload; registering here would double up). Result arms are groundwork for B-2026-07-21-15 — harmless while the struct drop skips Result fields, load-bearing the moment it arms. Verified: full o1-o6/a1 probe set + ro1/c2 controls clean (interp/JIT/AOT-O2/AOT-O0 parity, valgrind), c1 keeps the open -15 leak (no move in it — out of scope), full B-14 battery re-run green through the new let leg. Suites: codegen 2536/0, memory_sanitizer 828/0 (Linux LSan), interpreter 1283/0, clippy --all-targets clean, fmt clean. E2E test_e2e_owned_struct_optres_field_consume_and_move + LSan/ASAN asan_owned_struct_optres_field_consume_no_leak_no_double_free added. Note on semantics: the zero makes a consuming destructure of a struct field a MOVE (later reads of the field see None/empty), matching the established #15/#16 move-suppression semantics for every other field class.

title: A runtime error raised inside a spliced gated-stdlib wrapper body reports the USER file's path with the SPLICE-COMPOSITE line/col — `import std.lazy.{lit}; lit(vec![1i64])` errors at `<user file>:19:5` when the user file has 6 lines (line 19 is the wrapper body's position in the composite spliced source, not a location in the file named).
detail: SOURCE NOTE: found probing LazyExpr.lit's non-scalar rejection during LazyDataFrame slice 6 (constant folding). The error MESSAGE is right and loud ('LazyExpr.lit expects a scalar literal (i64 / f64 / String / bool), got Array'); only the LOCATION is misattributed. Mechanism: gated stdlib modules (runtime/stdlib/lazy.kara et al.) splice their source into a composite before evaluation, and a runtime error whose span falls inside the spliced wrapper keeps the composite offset while the path renders as the user file. Any real-bodied gated wrapper that can raise at runtime hits this class (std.lazy's `lit` is just the first with a runtime-only failure mode — `col` cannot fail, and most gated wrappers delegate to typechecker-guarded builtins). FIX DIRECTION: either remap spans landing in spliced segments to the stdlib module's own pseudo-path (`<std.lazy>:14:5`), or attribute the error to the USER call site span (the arg expression) rather than the wrapper body.
resolution: Fixed (5d5361f). Root cause confirmed as filed, with one refinement: gated imports splice AST items cloned from each module's own SEPARATE parse (GATED_STDLIB_PROGRAMS), so spans inside a wrapper body carry the MODULE source's line/col (lazy.kara line 19 for `lit`'s body), and the CLI renders every runtime-error span against the user file's path — there is no textual composite. Fix takes the filing's second direction (attribute to the USER call site — the faulting argument's home — rather than a stdlib pseudo-path, which would have needed per-span module provenance Span does not carry): the interpreter keeps a stack of active spliced-wrapper call-site spans, pushed around the Value::Function body eval in eval_call when the callee item is stdlib_origin; record_runtime_error attributes a raise to the OUTERMOST frame when the stack is non-empty, so nested wrapper-to-wrapper calls still point at the user-visible entry call, and the error return trace picks up the same span. The stack pops on return: plain user-code errors and errors after a successful wrapper call keep their raise-site spans (both probed). Repro now reports `at <user>:5:13` (the lit(...) call) on error and trace. KNOWN RESIDUALS (same class, other executors — none observable today): spliced IMPL-block methods dispatch through eval_method_call (current gated impl methods cannot raise), and a user closure invoked INSIDE a wrapper would attribute its error to the wrapper call (no closure-taking gated wrapper exists). Suites: interpreter 1287/0 + new test, cli 490/0, codegen 2537/0, memory_sanitizer 830/0, clippy --all-targets clean, fmt clean. Test: test_stdlib_wrapper_runtime_error_attributed_to_call_site.

title: `if let Some(n) = v.pop()` / `while let` / `let…else` over a `Vec[shared T]` (or `VecDeque`) LEAKS the popped node — every element popped through a NON-`match` binding form leaks its refcount. `Vec.pop` is an INLINED builtin whose result is an owned `Option[shared T]` rvalue (the container relinquishes the slot via `len--`, transferring its +1 to the returned Option), but that inlined result never rides the general owned-temp-drop registration a real fn/method return does. The `match` scrutinee chain registered `track_freshtemp_shared_option_scrutinee` for exactly this shape; `if let`/`while let`/`let…else` stopped at the boxed-enum tracker, so the pattern bind's rc-inc + scope-exit rc-dec cancel and the transferred +1 orphans → the node is never freed. Codegen-only (interp/JIT correct output; AOT valgrind + Linux LSan). `match v.pop()` and `if let Some(x) = make()` (real fn/method return, caught by `materialize_freshtemp_enum_scrutinee`) were both already CLEAN.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: found by a valgrind/LSan leak sweep of the 78 shared/RC/weak-using leetcode katas (post-#133-fix regression audit). 2 katas leaked from this bug — #94 binary-tree-inorder-traversal/inorder_iter (explicit `Vec[TreeNode]` stack, `if let Some(node) = stack.pop()`) and #23 merge-k-sorted-lists/heap (`if let Some(x) = heap.pop()`); a 3rd finding, #133 clone-graph, is the SEPARATE by-design undirected-graph RC cycle (all-strong `Vec[Node]` neighbors, design.md 8856, safe/expected like Rust `Rc`). ROOT CAUSE: `compile_match` (control_flow_match.rs) runs a mutually-exclusive fresh-temp scrutinee tracker chain — materialize_freshtemp_enum_scrutinee (#1) → track_freshtemp_boxed_enum_scrutinee (#2) → track_freshtemp_inline_result_scrutinee (#3) → track_freshtemp_shared_option_scrutinee (#4), each gated on the prior returning None. For an `Option[shared T]` scrutinee #1/#2/#3 return None and #4 registers the RcDecOption cleanup that releases the transferred ref. `compile_if_let` / `compile_while_let` / `compile_let_else` (control_flow.rs) only ran #1 and #2, so #4 never fired for a non-`match` pop → leak. A real fn/method return was caught by #1 (its `Option[shared]` return type instance carries the droppable payload kind), which is why only the INLINED-builtin pop shape leaked and misdirected the bisection toward match-vs-if-let. FIX: mirror trackers #3 and #4 (with identical is-none gating) into all three non-match binding sites, and generalize `track_freshtemp_shared_option_scrutinee` to take `&[&Pattern]` (was `&[MatchArm]`) so if-let/while-let/let-else pass their single pattern. Verified: minimal pop (if-let/while-let/let-else) LSan-clean; #94 + #23 CLEAN; fn/method-return (`make()`, `b.make()`) still CLEAN (no double-free — #1 still owns those); #133 unchanged (its 240-byte residual is the true cycle). Regression test: tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_iflet_pop_shared_struct_no_leak.
resolution: mirrored the match scrutinee's fresh-temp inline-Result + shared-Option cleanup trackers into compile_if_let/compile_while_let/compile_let_else; refactored track_freshtemp_shared_option_scrutinee to a patterns slice.

title: A nested `Option[shared]` FIELD reachable ONLY through a popped node leaks when the container is drained via `if let`/`while let`/`let…else` (but NOT `match`). `shared struct N { val: i64, mut nxt: Option[N] }`; `st: Vec[N]` holds node A with `A.nxt = Some(B)` where B is referenced nowhere else; `if let Some(n) = st.pop() { … }` frees A but leaks B (24 bytes). `match st.pop() { Some(n) => … }` is CLEAN (B freed). Build-and-drop without any pop is also CLEAN. So the container drop and the match path both recurse into the popped node's nested shared field; the three non-match binding forms do not. Codegen-only; AOT valgrind/LSan (output correct on all surfaces).
detail: SOURCE NOTE: uncovered during B-2026-07-21-18 post-fix verification (the same 78-kata shared/RC leak sweep). DISTINCT from -18 and NOT a regression from it: measured 24 bytes leaked BOTH pre-fix (587dc94^) and post-fix (587dc94) for the nested if-let case, whereas the FLAT case (`shared struct N { val: i64 }`, no nested field) went 16 bytes → CLEAN with -18. So -18 (flat popped-node leak) and this (nested inner-field leak) are independent. NOT hit by the current corpus: #94 inorder_iter and #23 merge-k both push AND pop every shared node individually, so no node is reachable solely via a popped parent's field — they verified LSan-clean after -18. This bug bites only the narrower shape where an inner shared node lives exclusively behind a popped node's `Option[shared]` field. ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESIS (unconfirmed): for an `Option[shared]` whose shared payload type has droppable fields, tracker #1 `materialize_freshtemp_enum_scrutinee` (→ track_enum_var) CLAIMS the freshtemp scrutinee in both match and if-let (so -18's tracker #4 never runs here), but the popped node's recursive field-drop only completes on the match arm; the if-let/while-let/let-else lowering either suppresses the payload's nested-field cleanup after binding, or frees the popped box without recursing into its `Option[shared]` field. FIX DIRECTION: compare compile_if_let/while_let/let_else's post-bind payload-cleanup handling against compile_match's Some-arm for a shared payload with a nested droppable field; ensure the popped node's drop path is the recursive rc-dec (emit_refcount_dec → __karac_rc_drop_N) in all four binding forms. Candidate: route Option[shared] scrutinees uniformly through the shared-option tracker (#4, recursive) instead of letting #1 claim them — but that touches the match path (currently clean), so guard against a match-path regression.
resolution: WITHDRAWN 2026-07-21 — NOT REPRODUCIBLE on main (391f7a0); measurement artifact from a STALE pre-rebase binary, not a real defect. Re-verified on a clean-built HEAD binary across a broad nested-shape battery — if-let / while-let / let-else / match, 1-to-3-level `Option[N]` chains, `VecDeque.pop_front`, and the inner-node-accessed variant — every case reports valgrind 'All heap blocks were freed -- no leaks are possible' under AOT (KARAC_AUTO_PAR=0), including the exact repros in the title. ROOT CAUSE OF THE FALSE ALARM: the 24/48-byte figures were read from the binary that produced the original B-2026-07-21-18 finding, which was compiled BEFORE the concurrent 2026-07-21 codegen fixes landing on main during B-18's rebase (b65ebf1 route-JIT-to-interp, f9be2c7 = B-2026-07-21-16 owned-place Option/Result field move-site zeroing, et al.) were integrated into the COMPILED binary. The rebase merged those commits into the source tree but karac was not relinked between the rebase and the leak measurement, so the numbers reflect my-B18-on-the-old-base, not integrated main. Once main was rebuilt with everything integrated, the nested leak was gone. IR cross-check confirms if-let and match emit an identical drop set (one binding __karac_rc_drop_N, one RcDecOption __karac_rc_drop_N on the freshtemp shared-opt inner ptr, the vec-element rc-dec, and the buffer frees) — there is no if-let-vs-match divergence to fix. No compiler change made; the B-18 fix (587dc94, in main) is what makes the shared-Option pop path count-correct for the nested payload too. Lesson logged: always rebuild karac after a rebase before trusting an AOT leak measurement.

title: Spurious E0500 UseAfterMove on a weak-to-weak field splice `nodes[i].next = nodes[prev].next` where both sides index the SAME `Vec[shared]` and `next` is a `weak` field. `karac check` reports "value 'nodes' moved here, used again here" (moved at the RHS `nodes[prev].next` read), but `karac build`/`run` compile and run it CORRECTLY and valgrind-clean (the reuse takes the advisory shared/RC fallback). The false-positive is specific to the extra `weak`-field access on the RHS: the strong-element form `nodes[i].next = nodes[prev]` (the #143 shape) is NOT flagged. Advisory only — ownership errors other than aliased exclusive borrows do not block the build.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: surfaced authoring leetcode #147 (insertion sort on a Vec-owned + `weak next` list, the #141/#142/#143 cluster). The canonical insertion-sort splice `node.next = prev.next; prev.next = node` lowers to `nodes[i].next = nodes[prev].next` — both sides indexing `nodes: Vec[Node]`, RHS reading the `weak next` field. MINIMAL REPRO (E0500): `shared struct Node { val: i64, id: i64, mut next: weak Node } fn main() { let mut nodes: Vec[Node] = Vec.new(); nodes.push(Node{val:1i64,id:0i64,next:None}); nodes.push(Node{val:2i64,id:1i64,next:None}); nodes[0].next = nodes[1].next; }`. CONTROL (clean, no E0500): replace the last line with `nodes[0].next = nodes[1];` (strong element RHS). So the classifier treats the indexed `weak`-FIELD read on the RHS as a Consume of the container `nodes`, colliding with the LHS index-assign into the same `nodes`; a bare indexed-element read is correctly non-consuming. Sibling of B-2026-07-19-3 (the owned-param `f(nums); f(nums)` UseAfterMove false-positive that got a machine-applicable `.clone()` auto-fix); this is a distinct trigger (compound index-assign self-reference via a weak-field read, no clean `.clone()` insertion site). FIX DIRECTION: in the use classifier, an indexed `<place>[idx].<weakfield>` read on the RHS of an index-assign into the same place root must classify as a non-consuming read (like the bare-element case), not a Consume. Kata #147 keeps the natural weak-to-weak splice and documents the advisory diagnostic (README), matching #137's treatment of the same error class.

title: Materializing a `weak`-field read into a strong `Option[shared]` local and then storing it back into another `weak` field DOUBLE-FREES / use-after-frees. `let after: Option[Node] = nodes[prev].next; nodes[i].next = after;` (`next` is a `weak Node` field) `check`s clean but leaks the upgraded node (40 bytes for a single splice) and, across multiple such splices, corrupts the heap — valgrind reports 'Invalid read of size 8' (use-after-free) and the fuller workload aborts with 'free(): double free detected'. The weak->strong upgrade rc-incs the node, but the subsequent store of that strong `Option` into a `weak` field does not balance against the temp's scope-exit drop, so the node is over-released and later reads/frees hit freed memory. Codegen-only (interp/JIT output correct); AOT valgrind + glibc double-free abort.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: surfaced authoring leetcode #147 as the STRONG-round-trip alternative to the weak-to-weak splice of B-2026-07-21-20. When the E0500 false-positive (-20) pushes an author toward the 'save the successor first' idiom `let after = prev.next; node.next = after; prev.next = node;`, that materializes the `weak` read as a strong `Option[Node]` and stores it back into a `weak` field — which MISCOMPILES. MINIMAL REPRO (leak, 1 splice): `shared struct Node { val: i64, id: i64, mut next: weak Node } fn main() { let mut nodes: Vec[Node] = Vec.new(); nodes.push(Node{val:1i64,id:0i64,next:None}); nodes.push(Node{val:2i64,id:1i64,next:None}); nodes.push(Node{val:3i64,id:2i64,next:None}); nodes[0].next = nodes[1]; let after: Option[Node] = nodes[0].next; nodes[2].next = after; }` -> 'definitely lost: 40 bytes'. ESCALATED REPRO (use-after-free / double-free): two materialize-and-store splices in sequence (`let a = nodes[0].next; nodes[2].next = a; let b = nodes[1].next; nodes[2].next = b;`) -> valgrind 'Invalid read of size 8' x2; the full #147 kata written this way aborts 'free(): double free detected in tcache 2'. ROOT CAUSE (hypothesis): the weak-field read upgrades to a strong `Option[shared]` (rc-inc, correct), but storing that strong `Option` into a `weak`-typed destination field must DOWNGRADE (store a weak ref, drop the strong upgrade's +1) — codegen instead appears to move/retain the strong ref into the weak slot AND still drop the temp, netting an over-release. Contrast: the weak-to-weak direct copy `nodes[i].next = nodes[prev].next` (no strong materialization) is memory-correct and valgrind-clean (that is what kata #147 ships). FIX DIRECTION: at a strong `Option[shared]`/`shared` -> `weak field` store, emit a downgrade (weak store, no retained strong count) so the source temp's scope-exit dec balances the upgrade; audit the assign-into-weak-field lowering for the strong-RHS case.

title:
test_e2e_f16_bf16_enum_payload_pack_unpack CRASHES on macOS arm64 (empty stdout vs expected '4
1
3.5
1.75

') while Linux arm64 AND x86 are green — the B-2026-07-20-12 f16-in-compound-enum-payload fix does not hold on Mach-O/Apple arm64; CI leg 'Codegen E2E (macOS arm64)' red across all recent main runs.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: CI triage 2026-07-22 (cloud session A) while landing the LazyFrame codegen twin — failure predates the lazy work (red back through 391f7a0 at least). Empty stdout = the built binary aborts/crashes before printing (wrong-value was the ORIGINAL B-2026-07-20-12 symptom; this is the harder sibling). Linux-arm64 E2E leg is GREEN, so it is Apple-arm64-specific (Mach-O / Darwin ABI or alignment), not generic aarch64 — half-float (f16/bf16) enum payload pack/unpack lowering differs somewhere Apple-specific (calling convention for half types on Darwin arm64 is a known compiler pain point). NOT reproducible in the Linux x86 dev container — needs a macOS arm64 machine or the CI leg itself for iteration. Cross-ref: B-2026-07-20-12 (fixed — x86-verified).

title: asan_closure_captures_heap_struct_returned_clean FAILS on the arm64 LSan CI leg (memory-sanitizer-arm64) while the x86 LSan leg is green — an arm64-ONLY leak (or arm64-only asan report) in the closure-captures-heap-struct-returned path; red across all recent main runs.
detail: SOURCE NOTE: CI triage 2026-07-22 (cloud session A); predates the LazyFrame twin work. The proof-of-existence precedent for arch-dependent leaks is B-2026-07-12-29 (shared-Vec index-assign leaked on arm64 only, balanced on x86 — fixed 63d618e); this is the same CI-leg split. The test is recent (closure/ref-chain fix series, B-2026-07-21-*). Repro needs an arm64 Linux box (scripts/lsan-local.sh is native arm64 per CLAUDE.md — a local-Mac session can run it) or the CI leg. Per CLAUDE.md the memory-sanitizer-arm64 job is non-required but is the PERMANENT gate for the arm64 leak class — do not close any closure-capture leak-class fix on x86 evidence alone.
